NIST-1000.description: Provide security and privacy policies for all applicable security control families or domains. Each policy must include purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, and compliance requirements, with documented management approval and revision history confirming the policy is reviewed and updated at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, and following significant changes. Additionally, provide evidence confirming each policy is disseminated to applicable personnel or roles — such as distribution records, intranet or portal publishing confirmation, all-staff notification records, or equivalent accessibility evidence.

NIST-1001.description: Provide security and privacy procedures for all applicable security control families or domains. Each set of procedures must be tied to the corresponding approved policy, reviewed and updated at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements and following significant changes, and include designation records identifying the official responsible for managing their development, documentation, and dissemination. Additionally, provide evidence confirming each set of procedures is disseminated to applicable personnel or roles.

NIST-1002.description: Provide ConMon records confirming risk monitoring is integrated as an integral component of the continuous monitoring strategy — not a separate activity — with evidence that monitoring outputs feed into risk management decisions. Risk monitoring must demonstrably incorporate all three sub-elements: (1) effectiveness monitoring — ongoing assessment of control effectiveness with results informing risk decisions, such as assessment findings or control performance metrics from the current ConMon period; (2) compliance monitoring — active tracking of compliance posture against defined requirements, with compliance status records from the current period; and (3) change monitoring — tracking of system and environmental changes that could affect risk posture, with change records showing risk impact was evaluated.

NIST-1003.description: Provide the system-level continuous monitoring strategy confirming it is developed and implemented in alignment with the organizational ConMon strategy, with current approval and revision history.

NIST-1004.description: Provide continuous monitoring execution records confirming ongoing control assessments are being performed at the frequencies defined in the ConMon strategy, with assessment results from the current period.

NIST-1005.description: Provide ongoing monitoring records confirming system and organizational metrics are tracked at defined frequencies, with metric results from the current period showing the monitoring program is active.

NIST-1006.description: Provide the Configuration Management Plan confirming it is developed, documented, and implemented — not just referenced in the SSP — and addresses all required elements: (1) CM roles, responsibilities, and the processes and procedures governing CM activities; (2) a defined process for identifying configuration items throughout the SDLC; (3) a defined process for managing the configuration of identified CIs, including change control and version management; (4) the specific system components designated as CIs subject to CM controls; (5) confirmation that defined CIs are actively placed under CM — not just listed; (6) current review and approval with designated personnel signatures and approval date; and (7) the plan is protected against unauthorized disclosure and modification — with access controls, need-to-know restrictions, and distribution limited to personnel with CM responsibilities confirmed.

NIST-1007.description: Provide the Contingency Plan along with records confirming it was coordinated with personnel responsible for related organizational plans — such as the incident response plan and business continuity plan — with coordination documented prior to approval.

NIST-1008.description: Provide the Contingency Plan confirming it defines the timeframe within which essential missions and business functions will resume following activation, consistent with recovery time objectives.

NIST-1009.description: Provide the Contingency Plan confirming it addresses all required elements: (1) essential mission and business functions and their contingency requirements; (2) recovery objectives, restoration priorities, and measurable metrics; (3) contingency roles, responsibilities, and assigned individuals with current contact information; (4) how essential functions will be maintained during a disruption, compromise, or failure; (5) eventual full-system restoration without degradation of the originally planned security controls; and (6) how contingency information will be shared with relevant stakeholders during an activation. Include current approval signatures and revision history.

NIST-1010.description: Provide the Contingency Plan along with the approval record confirming it has been reviewed and approved by designated personnel or roles with current approval signatures and dates.

NIST-1011.description: Provide distribution records confirming copies of the Contingency Plan were provided to key contingency personnel by name and/or role, with the distribution list and confirmation of receipt.

NIST-1012.description: Provide distribution records confirming copies of the Contingency Plan were distributed to key contingency organizational elements, with evidence of receipt.

NIST-1013.description: Provide the most recent Contingency Plan review record confirming the plan was reviewed at least annually, with the review date, reviewer identity, and any updates resulting from the review documented.

NIST-1014.description: Provide records confirming the Contingency Plan was updated to address changes to the organization, system, or operational environment — with the triggering change and corresponding plan update documented.

NIST-1015.description: Provide records confirming the Contingency Plan was updated to address problems identified during contingency plan testing or actual activations, with lessons learned incorporated.

NIST-1016.description: Provide records confirming Contingency Plan changes were communicated to key contingency personnel and organizational elements after each update, with distribution evidence.

NIST-1017.description: Provide records confirming lessons learned from contingency training, testing, or actual activations are incorporated into updated contingency testing and training materials.

NIST-1018.description: Provide access control records for the Contingency Plan repository confirming the plan is protected from unauthorized disclosure and modification, with RBAC or equivalent controls in place.

NIST-1019.description: Provide contingency training content review records confirming training materials are reviewed and updated at least annually to reflect current contingency plan requirements, lessons learned, and any plan changes — and also following defined triggering events such as an actual contingency activation or a significant plan revision — with the review date, triggering condition, and any resulting content changes documented for each review.

NIST-1020.description: Provide records confirming the contingency test was coordinated with organizational elements responsible for related plans — such as incident response and continuity of operations — prior to execution.

NIST-1021.description: Provide contingency plan test or exercise records confirming the plan is tested at least annually per FedRAMP requirements, with tests developed per NIST SP 800-34 guidelines. Both the test plan and executed results must be available.

NIST-1022.description: Provide contingency test results confirming they are included in the security package submitted to FedRAMP, with results showing whether recovery objectives were met and any deficiencies identified.

NIST-1023.description: Provide incident handling records from the current period confirming the FISMA definition of 'incident' is applied — any occurrence that actually or imminently jeopardizes the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of information or systems — with detection, containment, eradication, and recovery documented per the IR plan.

NIST-1024.description: Provide distribution records confirming copies of the Incident Response Plan are provided to all defined incident response personnel and organizational elements, with evidence of receipt or access confirmation for each recipient.

NIST-1025.description: Provide IRP update records confirming the plan is reviewed and revised to address system and organizational changes, and that problems encountered during plan implementation, execution, or testing are incorporated — with the triggering change and corresponding update documented.

NIST-1026.description: Provide records confirming incident response plan changes are communicated to all defined incident response personnel and organizational elements following each plan update, with distribution records or notification evidence showing recipients and communication dates.

NIST-1027.description: Provide the access controls for the IRP repository confirming the plan is protected from unauthorized disclosure and modification, with RBAC or equivalent controls restricting who can view and edit the document.

NIST-1028.description: Provide information spillage response training completion records confirming applicable personnel receive spillage-specific training at least annually per FedRAMP requirements, with training content, completion dates, and participant records available.

NIST-1029.description: Provide the System Security Plan (SSP) confirming all required sections are fully populated and current. Evidence must show: (1) security categorization of the system with supporting rationale; (2) boundary definition with all components identified; (3) information types processed, stored, and transmitted by the system; (4) any specific threats to the system that are of concern to the organization; (5) operational environment for the system and any dependencies on or connections to other systems or system components; (6) relevant control baselines or overlays applied, if applicable; (7) each control implementation statement addresses how the control is met — not templated or placeholder text; (8) current authorizing official approval with signature and date; and (9) revision history confirming the SSP was reviewed within the required frequency and updated to reflect any system changes.

NIST-1030.description: Provide the Privacy Plan confirming all required privacy program elements are addressed and current. Evidence must show: (1) the privacy roles and responsibilities are defined; (2) privacy controls are addressed with implementation detail — not placeholder text; (3) the results of a privacy risk assessment for systems processing personally identifiable information; (4) current management approval with signature and date; and (5) revision history confirming the plan was reviewed within the required frequency and reflects current system operations.

NIST-1031.description: Provide records confirming planned security and privacy architecture changes are reflected in the current security and privacy plans — with plan update records, revision history, or change-specific annotations showing that each significant architecture change has been incorporated into the plan.

NIST-1032.description: Provide records confirming planned architecture changes are reflected in the organization's Concept of Operations (CONOPS), criticality analysis, organizational procedures, and procurement or acquisition documentation — with update dates and change-specific references confirming each artifact was updated following significant architecture changes.

NIST-1033.description: Provide the SCRM plan confirming it addresses risks associated with the full system lifecycle — research, development, design, manufacturing, acquisition, delivery, integration, operation, and disposal — for all defined systems, components, and services.

NIST-1034.description: Provide SCRM plan review records confirming the plan is reviewed and updated at least annually or whenever significant threat, organizational, or environmental changes occur.

NIST-1035.description: Provide access control records for the SCRM plan repository confirming the plan is protected from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

NIST-1036.description: Provide the anti-counterfeit program documentation and operational records confirming: (1) an anti-counterfeit policy and associated procedures are developed, implemented, and current — covering organizational responsibilities and counterfeit identification processes; (2) the procedures explicitly include the means to detect and prevent counterfeit components from entering the system, with specific detection and prevention methods described; and (3) identified counterfeit components are reported to defined sources, external reporting organizations, and relevant personnel — with a sample reporting record as evidence.

NIST-1038.description: Provide the account management records confirming designated account managers are assigned by name and role. Include a screenshot from the system or authoritative source showing account managers are actively identified — not just a policy statement asserting they exist.

NIST-1039.description: Provide the prerequisites and criteria governing group and role membership, with screenshot evidence from the system confirming conditions are enforced prior to provisioning. The evidence must show the criteria — not just that accounts are in place.

NIST-1040.description: Provide the account specification documentation confirming that for each system account, the following are defined and recorded: the authorized user(s) assigned; group and role memberships; access authorizations (privileges) granted; and any required attributes — such as security clearance level, organizational affiliation, or need-to-know — consistent with CSP-defined account requirements.

NIST-1041.description: Provide access management records covering each account lifecycle action — creation, enabling, modification, disabling, and removal. For each action, provide the actual ticket or record showing: the requestor's name and title, the designated approver's name and title, the specific access granted or changed, and the date — confirming approval precedes provisioning. Each record must also document the authorization basis: (1) a valid access authorization is established; (2) the intended system usage is documented; and (3) access is consistent with defined user attributes such as role, clearance level, or need-to-know. In addition, provide system configuration evidence confirming: approved access authorizations are technically enforced across all in-scope system resources; the principle of least privilege is applied — users and processes have only the minimum access necessary for their assigned tasks with no undocumented or excess permissions; and privileged access is revoked promptly when role or attribute assignments are no longer appropriate, with a sample revocation record from the current period confirming timely execution.

NIST-1042.description: Provide account usage monitoring records from the current period confirming account activity is reviewed and atypical usage is detected and reported.

NIST-1043.description: Provide records confirming account managers and designated personnel are notified within defined timeframes when accounts are no longer required — within 24 hours of separation or transfer, and within 8 hours for privileged account events — with sample notification records as evidence.

NIST-1044.description: Provide account review records confirming privileged and non-privileged accounts are reviewed at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, with the most recent review records, reviewer identity, scope of accounts reviewed, and documentation of any access modifications resulting from each review.

NIST-1045.description: Provide the shared or group account governance records. Even if no group accounts are currently in use, document the defined process for how they would be managed if needed — including the conditions for approval, justification requirements, and how authenticators would be changed when group membership changes.

NIST-1046.description: Provide records confirming account management is aligned with personnel termination and transfer processes.

NIST-1047.description: Provide the automated account management configuration confirming accounts are created, modified, disabled, and removed through automated mechanisms. Include system-generated records from the current period showing the automation is active — not just that the feature is configured.

NIST-1048.description: Provide configuration records confirming temporary and emergency accounts are automatically disabled after no more than an organization-defined period, consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, with configuration output from all applicable systems confirming the threshold is enforced.

NIST-1049.description: Provide configuration records and sample evidence confirming: (1) system access is revoked promptly upon notification of personnel transfer or separation, within the timeframe required by the applicable framework; and (2) accounts showing no activity for an organization-defined period are automatically disabled. Include configuration output and a sample of recently disabled inactive accounts.

NIST-1050.description: Provide system-generated account activity logs from the current period confirming automated audit and notification actions fire for account creation, modification, enabling, disabling, and removal. Each action type should have a corresponding log record — not a single generic 'notifications are configured' statement.

NIST-1051.description: Provide session termination configuration records confirming the system automatically terminates or disconnects inactive sessions after no more than an organization-defined period of inactivity, consistent with the applicable framework's requirements. Include configuration output from all applicable systems and interfaces confirming the timeout is enforced.

NIST-1052.description: Provide privileged account management records confirming: (1) privileged accounts are established and administered in accordance with the defined role-based scheme, with account justification and approval documented; (2) privileged role and attribute assignments are actively monitored, with current assignment records maintained; and (3) changes to privileged roles or attributes are detected and logged — with monitoring configuration and a sample change log from the current period.

NIST-1053.description: Provide shared and group account records confirming use is limited to defined conditions with documented justification. If no shared or group accounts are currently deployed, do not mark this N/A — provide the documented process that would govern their use if the need arises, including approval requirements and authenticator management procedures.

NIST-1054.description: Provide the system account configuration confirming usage conditions and circumstances are defined and enforced for applicable system accounts — specifying constraints such as time-of-day restrictions, location-based access controls, or device-type limitations — with configuration output confirming the conditions are actively enforced.

NIST-1055.description: Provide the account monitoring configuration and a sample monitoring report confirming: (1) system accounts are monitored for atypical usage patterns — such as unusual access times, geographic anomalies, or volume spikes; and (2) when atypical usage is detected, it is automatically reported to defined personnel or roles — with a sample alert or notification from the current period.

NIST-1056.description: Provide records confirming accounts of individuals identified as a significant risk are disabled within one (1) hour of discovery per FedRAMP requirements, with a ticket or log confirming timely execution.

NIST-1058.description: Provide information flow diagrams and enforcement configuration demonstrating how approved information flows are controlled across the system boundary. Specifically address: (1) how customer data flows are logically separated from other tenants' data flows; and (2) how management and administrative traffic is separated from CSP user data flows. Reference SC-7.c managed interfaces as the enforcement points.

NIST-1059.description: Provide the data flow control configuration confirming encrypted information is prevented from bypassing defined information flow control mechanisms — with intrusion detection mechanisms or equivalent controls in place to enforce this restriction, consistent with M-21-31 requirements — and configuration evidence confirming encrypted traffic cannot circumvent flow controls.

NIST-1060.description: Provide the information flow separation configuration confirming data flows are logically or physically separated using defined mechanisms — such as virtual network segmentation or dedicated processing environments — to achieve required separations between tenants, classifications, or data types.

NIST-1061.description: Provide the separation of duties documentation confirming all defined individual duties are identified and documented — specifically, duties that must be separated to prevent any single person from completing a sensitive operation without oversight. A separation of duties matrix may be provided as a supporting artifact per FedRAMP guidance.

NIST-1062.description: Provide the access configuration confirming users are authorized to access only the specific security functions — including all hardware, software, and firmware security functions not publicly accessible — and security-relevant information required for their defined role, with no undocumented or excess authorizations granted.

NIST-1063.description: Provide the system configuration confirming non-privileged accounts require explicit authorization for security functions — including establishing accounts, configuring permissions, setting security parameters, and installing software — with no undocumented non-privileged access to these functions.

NIST-1064.description: Provide records confirming: (1) network access to all privileged commands is authorized only for defined compelling operational needs, with each authorization documented; and (2) the rationale for authorizing network access to privileged commands is documented in the system security plan.

NIST-1065.description: Provide the privileged account listing confirming privileged access is restricted to designated personnel or roles only, with no undocumented privileged accounts.

NIST-1066.description: Provide privilege review records confirming: (1) privileges assigned to defined roles and classes are reviewed at the defined frequency, with the review date, reviewer identity, and scope documented; and (2) following each review, privileges are reassigned or removed as necessary to correctly reflect current organizational mission requirements and individual need-to-know.

NIST-1067.description: Provide the software execution privilege configuration confirming: (1) any software not explicitly documented as requiring elevated privileges is prevented from executing at higher privilege levels than the users running it; and (2) all software permitted to execute does so only with the minimum privileges necessary to accomplish authorized tasks — with application privilege settings, execution controls, or allowlist/denylist configurations confirming both least-privilege enforcement and elevated-privilege prevention are active across all applicable systems.

NIST-1068.description: Provide the audit configuration and log records confirming all privileged function executions are captured in the audit trail.

NIST-1069.description: Provide the system configuration confirming non-privileged accounts are technically blocked from executing privileged functions — with role or permission configuration evidence showing the enforcement mechanism is active.

NIST-1070.description: Provide the account lockout configuration confirming: (1) the consecutive invalid logon attempt limit is set to the defined value — for DoD IL5, no more than three (3) consecutive unsuccessful attempts for privileged accounts, with administrator unlock required; (2) the organization-defined lockout action (account/node lock for a defined period, account lock until released by administrator, or delay of next logon prompt) is enforced; (3) the lockout configuration applies consistently across all system access interfaces — with system configuration output confirming DoD IL5 lockout parameters are applied per DoDI 8520.03.

NIST-1071.description: Provide a screenshot of the system use notification as displayed to users before access is granted, confirming it includes privacy and security notices consistent with applicable laws, Executive Orders, directives, regulations, policies, and standards. Include evidence the Authorizing Official has approved the notification content and the verification periodicity.

NIST-1072.description: Provide a screenshot of the system use notification as displayed to users at login, confirming it states: the system is a U.S. Government system; usage may be monitored, recorded, and audited; unauthorized use is prohibited and subject to penalties; and use constitutes consent to monitoring.

NIST-1073.description: Provide a system demonstration or screenshot confirming the system use notification or banner is retained on screen and requires users to take an explicit action — such as clicking 'Accept' or 'Agree' — before being granted access to the system. The banner must not be dismissible passively; access must be blocked until the user affirmatively acknowledges it.

NIST-1074.description: Provide records confirming publicly accessible system components display the required system use notification and that the content is reviewed and updated at the defined frequency.

NIST-1075.description: Provide the concurrent session configuration confirming the number of simultaneous sessions is limited to no more than three (3) sessions for privileged accounts and two (2) sessions for non-privileged accounts — with configuration output from all applicable systems confirming the limits are enforced.

NIST-1076.description: Provide session lock configuration records confirming: (1) the system initiates a session lock after no more than an organization-defined period of inactivity, concealing previously visible information; and (2) once a session lock is activated, re-access requires the user to re-establish their session using established identification and authentication procedures. Include configuration output confirming both the lock timeout and re-authentication requirement are enforced across all applicable systems and interfaces.

NIST-1077.description: Provide the screen lock configuration confirming that when a device lock is activated, all previously visible information on the display is concealed and replaced with a publicly viewable image — preventing shoulder-surfing of locked session content.

NIST-1078.description: Provide the session termination configuration confirming sessions are automatically terminated after the defined conditions are met — such as inactivity timeout or end of session — across all in-scope session types.

NIST-1081.description: Provide the documented list of user actions permitted without identification or authentication, confirming each is consistent with organizational mission needs and formally approved.

NIST-1085.description: Provide the remote access configuration confirming usage restrictions, connection requirements, and monitoring are established for every remote access method documented in the SSP — including VPN, remote desktop, SSH, cloud management console access, and any other remote access path in use. Each method must be individually addressed; a single generic statement covering all methods is not sufficient.

NIST-1086.description: Provide authorization records for each type of remote access in use. For each method, reference the specific authorization ticket confirming the user or role is approved to use that access path — including the requestor's name and title, the approver's name and title, and the date of approval.

NIST-1087.description: Provide the remote access monitoring configuration confirming automated mechanisms monitor and control all remote access methods, with log records from the current period confirming active monitoring is in place.

NIST-1088.description: Provide the remote access authorization records confirming: (1) execution of privileged commands via remote access is permitted only for defined operational needs, with justification documented and assessable evidence maintained; and (2) access to security-relevant information via remote access is authorized only for defined needs with justification and assessable evidence — with both the authorization records and the technical controls enforcing these restrictions available.

NIST-1092.description: Provide the wireless access policy and configuration standards confirming configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance are established and documented for each type of wireless access authorized — covering authentication, encryption, and access control requirements per wireless access type.

NIST-1093.description: Provide wireless access authorization records confirming each type of wireless connection is explicitly approved before being permitted, with the current list of authorized wireless access types available.

NIST-1094.description: Provide the wireless access configuration confirming all wireless connections are protected by both: (1) authentication of users, devices, or both before access is granted; and (2) encryption of all data in transit — with the authentication mechanism, encryption protocol, and cipher strength confirmed.

NIST-1095.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming when not intended for use, wireless networking capabilities embedded within system components are disabled prior to issuance and deployment.

NIST-1096.description: Provide records confirming individuals authorized to independently configure wireless networking capabilities are explicitly identified and authorized — with authorization records and the list of individuals or roles with wireless configuration permissions.

NIST-1097.description: Provide the wireless access point configuration confirming: (1) radio antennas are selected to reduce the probability that wireless signals can be received outside of organization-controlled boundaries; and (2) transmission power levels are calibrated to minimize signal propagation beyond defined perimeters — with RF survey results or equivalent evidence confirming signal containment.

NIST-1098.description: Provide the mobile device management configuration confirming requirements, connection standards, and implementation guidance are established for all organization-controlled mobile devices — including when devices are used outside of controlled areas — with MDM policy or equivalent documentation as evidence.

NIST-1099.description: Provide mobile device authorization records confirming only approved devices are permitted to connect to organizational systems.

NIST-1100.description: Provide the mobile device management configuration confirming full-device encryption or container-based encryption is enforced on all mobile devices that access organizational systems or data.

NIST-1101.description: Provide records confirming terms and conditions or identified security controls are established for authorized individuals using external systems to access organizational systems, consistent with trust relationships with other organizations.

NIST-1102.description: Provide records confirming the use of prohibited external system types is restricted or blocked, with evidence the restriction is enforced.

NIST-1103.description: Provide records confirming authorized individuals using external systems to access organizational systems or data have: (1) verified the external system employs required security controls before access is permitted; and (2) received approval from the appropriate authority prior to that use — with both verification and approval documented.

NIST-1104.description: Provide the portable storage device restriction configuration confirming use by authorized individuals on external systems is controlled per defined restrictions.

NIST-1106.description: Provide records confirming authorized users have a mechanism to determine whether a sharing partner's access authorizations match the information's access and use restrictions before sharing — with a description of how that determination is made.

NIST-1107.description: Provide the information-sharing decision support configuration confirming automated mechanisms or defined manual processes assist users in making appropriate information-sharing and collaboration decisions.

NIST-1108.description: Provide the access records and configuration confirming designated individuals are authorized to post information to publicly accessible systems, with the authorization list current.

NIST-1109.description: Provide training records confirming authorized individuals who post to publicly accessible systems receive training on how to ensure publicly accessible information does not contain non-public information — with training completion dates and content evidence confirming coverage of this requirement.

NIST-1110.description: Provide review records confirming proposed content is reviewed for non-public information before being posted to publicly accessible systems.

NIST-1111.description: Provide records confirming content on publicly accessible systems is reviewed at least quarterly for non-public information, and that any non-public information discovered is removed promptly — with the review date, reviewer identity, and any removals documented per review cycle.

NIST-1113.description: Provide security literacy training completion records confirming all users — including managers, senior executives, and contractors — completed training upon onboarding and at least annually thereafter.

NIST-1114.description: Provide privacy literacy training completion records confirming all users completed training upon onboarding and at least annually thereafter.

NIST-1115.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming security and privacy literacy training is provided to system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors) when required by system changes or following.

NIST-1116.description: Provide evidence confirming security and privacy awareness techniques are actively employed between formal training cycles — such as phishing simulations, security newsletters, policy acknowledgments, or security alerts — to reinforce user awareness on an ongoing basis.

NIST-1117.description: Provide training content review records confirming awareness training content is reviewed and updated at least annually and following significant changes.

NIST-1118.description: Provide records confirming lessons learned from internal or external security incidents or breaches are incorporated into both security and privacy literacy and awareness training and into role-based training. Evidence must show updated training content reflecting how specific lessons were applied — for awareness techniques and for role-based curricula — with the triggering incident or event and corresponding training update documented.

NIST-1119.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat is provided.

NIST-1120.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential and actual instances of social engineering and social mining is provided.

NIST-1124.description: Provide role-based security and privacy training completion records confirming applicable personnel completed training before being granted system access and at least annually thereafter.

NIST-1125.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming role-based security and privacy training is provided to personnel with assigned security roles and responsibilities when required by system changes.

NIST-1126.description: Provide role-based training content review records confirming training is updated at least annually and following significant events.

NIST-1129.description: Provide training program records confirming all information security and privacy training activities — including security and privacy awareness training and specific role-based training — are documented and actively monitored throughout the year, with completion rates, dates, and any gaps or exceptions recorded.

NIST-1130.description: Provide training record retention policy documentation and sample records confirming security and privacy training completion records are retained for at least the period required by the applicable framework. Include retention policy documentation and sample records confirming the retention period is enforced.

NIST-1132.description: Provide audit log records and the logging configuration confirming the required event types are audited for each identified event category — with the CSP-defined event types selected from the auditable events in AU-02, the events logged continuously, and the audit frequency for each event type documented. Provide evidence that audit logging is enabled for all in-scope components and that the event type selection is reviewed and updated when system or threat changes warrant.

NIST-1133.description: Provide records confirming the audit event selection process was coordinated with other organizational entities that require audit-related information — such as the security operations team or incident response function — with coordination documented.

NIST-1134.description: Provide audit log records confirming the specified event types are logged within the system.

NIST-1135.description: Provide the audit event selection rationale documentation, confirming coordination between the service provider and consumer is documented and accepted by the Authorizing Official — not just internally reviewed.

NIST-1136.description: Provide audit log records confirming the event types selected for logging are reviewed and updated.

NIST-1137.description: Provide audit log extracts confirming records contain all required fields: event type, timestamp, location or source, user or subject identity, and outcome (success or failure). Each field must be present and populated. Also confirm records include the additional FedRAMP-required fields: session or transaction duration, and for client-server transactions, the number of bytes sent and received in each direction. Provide system configuration evidence confirming all defined system components are generating audit records that contain these required content elements end-to-end.

NIST-1138.description: Provide the audit log storage configuration confirming capacity is allocated to accommodate the defined retention requirements, along with current storage utilization evidence showing capacity is not at risk of exhaustion.

NIST-1139.description: Provide the audit failure alerting and response configuration confirming: (1) defined personnel or roles are automatically alerted when audit logging processes fail; and (2) the system takes defined additional actions in response to audit logging failures — such as restricting system activity, overwriting oldest records, or shutting down — with a sample alert from the current period and the configured response actions documented.

NIST-1140.description: Provide the audit storage capacity alerting configuration confirming defined personnel, roles, or locations receive a warning within the defined time period when allocated audit log storage volume reaches the defined capacity threshold — with a sample warning from the current period confirming the alert mechanism is active.

NIST-1141.description: Provide the real-time audit failure alerting configuration confirming service provider personnel with authority to address failed audit events are alerted in real time when defined audit logging failure events requiring real-time alerts occur — with configuration evidence and a sample real-time alert confirming the capability is active.

NIST-1142.description: Provide audit review records from the current period confirming logs are reviewed and analyzed at least weekly per FedRAMP requirements for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity. In multi-tenant environments, include documentation confirming the capability and means for providing consumer-specific log review and reporting is in place.

NIST-1143.description: Provide the audit review reporting configuration and a sample report confirming findings from log analysis are reported to defined personnel or roles.

NIST-1144.description: Provide records confirming the level of audit record review, analysis, and reporting is adjusted when there is a change in organizational risk — such as based on law enforcement information, intelligence, or other threat indicators — with evidence that the review posture was modified in response to a specific risk change and that the adjustment is documented.

NIST-1145.description: Provide the automated audit review mechanism configuration confirming audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes are integrated through automated tools — such as a SIEM or log management platform — with evidence of automated correlation and alerting from the current period.

NIST-1146.description: Provide evidence confirming audit records from different repositories or systems are aggregated and correlated to support organization-wide situational awareness — such as a centralized SIEM configuration showing cross-source correlation is active.

NIST-1147.description: Provide the centralized audit review configuration confirming audit records from all network, data storage, and computing devices are compiled into a system-wide audit trail that is centrally reviewable and analyzable — with evidence the capability is implemented and operational.

NIST-1148.description: Provide records confirming audit record analysis is integrated with analysis of vulnerability scanning data, performance data, system monitoring information, and penetration test data — with evidence of cross-source correlation and any findings generated from integrated analysis during the review period.

NIST-1149.description: Provide records confirming information from audit records is correlated with physical access monitoring data to enhance detection of suspicious, inappropriate, unusual, or malevolent activity — with evidence of cross-correlation between logical and physical access events.

NIST-1150.description: Provide the audit permissions configuration confirming the permitted actions for each information system process, role, and user associated with audit record review, analysis, and reporting are explicitly specified and enforced — with access control records confirming audit function permissions are assigned and limited appropriately.

NIST-1151.description: Provide the audit management tool configuration confirming it provides: (1) audit record reduction and report generation capability supporting on-demand review, analysis, and reporting without altering the original content or time ordering of audit records; and (2) the ability to process, sort, and search audit records for events of interest based on defined fields within audit records — with a demonstration or output confirming both capabilities are active.

NIST-1152.description: Provide the audit log protection configuration confirming: (1) audit information and audit tools are protected from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion — with access permissions confirming only authorized roles can view or modify audit data; and (2) defined personnel or roles are automatically alerted upon detection of any unauthorized access attempt to audit information — with a sample alert from the current period.

NIST-1153.description: Provide the audit backup configuration confirming audit records are stored at least weekly to a repository on a physically different system or system component than the system or component being audited — with backup configuration and execution logs confirming the frequency and physical separation requirement are met.

NIST-1154.description: Provide the cryptographic integrity protection configuration for audit logs confirming FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms are used to protect the integrity of audit information and audit tools — with cryptographic configuration evidence and FIPS validation documentation.

NIST-1157.description: Provide the non-repudiation configuration confirming the system provides irrefutable evidence that individuals (or processes acting on their behalf) performed defined actions — including at minimum the addition, modification, deletion, approval, sending, or receiving of data — with configuration evidence and sample audit records demonstrating the capability.

NIST-1158.description: Provide audit log retention records confirming both storage tiers are in place in compliance with M-21-31: (1) active online storage — within the SIEM or logging platform — retaining at least 90 days of logs immediately accessible; and (2) long-term offline or archive storage retaining logs for a minimum of three years. Evidence for each tier must be shown separately with current retention configuration and utilization metrics.

NIST-1159.description: Provide the audit generation configuration confirming all defined system components — including all network devices, servers, and applications — have audit record generation capability enabled for the event types defined in AU-02.

NIST-1160.description: Provide the audit configuration confirming only designated personnel or roles are authorized to select and modify the event types logged by specific system components, with access controls restricting that capability to approved individuals.

NIST-1161.description: Provide the centralized audit trail configuration confirming audit records from all network, data storage, and computing devices are compiled into a system-wide audit trail that is time-correlated to within a defined tolerance — with configuration evidence confirming all components contribute to the unified, time-stamped trail.

NIST-1162.description: Provide the dynamic audit configuration confirming defined individuals or roles with audit configuration responsibilities have the capability to change the logging performed on all network, data storage, and computing devices based on defined selectable event criteria — with configuration records confirming dynamic audit capability is available and controlled.

NIST-1169.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming an appropriate 3PAO or equivalent independent assessor is selected for the type of assessment to be conducted — with the selection documented, the assessor's independence from the system owner confirmed, and the assessment scope and approach aligned with FedRAMP assessment requirements.

NIST-1170.description: Provide the control assessment plan confirming it explicitly identifies all controls and control enhancements within the scope of the assessment — with each in-scope control listed and no ambiguity about what is and is not being assessed.

NIST-1171.description: Provide the control assessment plan confirming it describes the assessment procedures to be used to determine control effectiveness — specifying the examine, interview, and test methods applicable to each in-scope control consistent with the assessment objectives.

NIST-1172.description: Provide the control assessment plan confirming it describes the assessment environment, the assessment team composition, and the roles and responsibilities of each team member throughout the assessment.

NIST-1173.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming the control assessment plan is reviewed and approved by the authorizing official or designated representative prior to conducting the assessment.

NIST-1174.description: Provide control assessment records confirming controls are assessed in the system and its environment of operation at least annually per FedRAMP requirements, to determine the extent to which the controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended, and producing the desired outcome with respect to meeting established security and privacy requirements.

NIST-1175.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming a control assessment report is produced that documents the results of the assessment.

NIST-1176.description: Provide records confirming control assessment results are provided to designated recipients, including FedRAMP.

NIST-1177.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming independent assessors or assessment teams are employed to conduct control assessments.

NIST-1178.description: Provide records confirming vulnerability scanning is conducted at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements as part of the security assessment or continuous monitoring process. Include assessment records confirming the scope, frequency, and inclusion of vulnerability scanning in the periodic assessment or monitoring cycle.

NIST-1179.description: Provide records confirming assessment results from independent assessors are leveraged when the assessment meets FedRAMP authorization requirements and is posted on the FedRAMP Marketplace.

NIST-1180.description: Provide the information exchange agreements — ISAs, ISEAs, or equivalent — confirming exchanges between the system and other systems are approved and managed with documented security requirements.

NIST-1181.description: Provide the interconnection security agreement (ISA) or equivalent exchange agreement confirming it formally documents for each connection: (1) interface characteristics; (2) security requirements; (3) privacy requirements; (4) controls implemented by each party; (5) responsibilities for each system; and (6) the impact level of the information communicated.

NIST-1182.description: Provide the most recent interconnection agreement review records confirming all system interconnection agreements are reviewed and updated at least annually, with the review date and any resulting changes documented.

NIST-1183.description: Provide records confirming individuals or systems transferring data between interconnected systems have the requisite authorizations — including write permissions or privileges — verified before accepting such data, with authorization check records or configuration confirming the verification is enforced.

NIST-1184.description: Provide the current POA&M confirming it documents planned remediation actions for all identified weaknesses or deficiencies — and that all open findings have assigned owners, target remediation dates, and documented remediation status — with the POA&M updated based on findings from control assessments, monitoring activities, and security incident reviews.

NIST-1185.description: Provide records confirming a senior official is designated as the authorizing official for the system and, separately, that a senior official is designated as the authorizing official for common controls available for inheritance by organizational systems — with current designations documented for both.

NIST-1186.description: Provide the ATO package or equivalent authorization documentation confirming that before commencing operations: (1) the authorizing official accepted the use of common controls inherited by the system; and (2) the authorizing official authorized the system to operate — with both decisions reflected in the authorization record.

NIST-1187.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming the authorizing official for common controls authorizes the use of those controls for inheritance by organizational systems.

NIST-1188.description: Provide records confirming system authorizations are reviewed and updated in accordance with OMB A-130 requirements or whenever a significant change occurs.

NIST-1189.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming system-level continuous monitoring includes establishment of the following system-level metrics to be monitored.

NIST-1190.description: Provide continuous monitoring execution records confirming configuration scans are conducted at least monthly for operating systems, databases, web applications, containers, and service configurations, and that all scans by independent assessors are performed at least annually per FedRAMP ConMon requirements.

NIST-1191.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming system-level continuous monitoring includes correlation and analysis of information generated by control assessments and monitoring.

NIST-1192.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming system-level continuous monitoring includes response actions to address the results of the analysis of control assessment and monitoring information.

NIST-1193.description: Provide ConMon status reporting records confirming the security and privacy status of the system is reported to defined personnel or roles at the CSP-defined frequency — with a sample status report or reporting record from the current period showing recipients, reporting date, and content.

NIST-1194.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming independent assessors or assessment teams are employed to monitor the controls in the system on an ongoing basis.

NIST-1198.description: Provide the most recent penetration test report confirming testing was conducted at least annually against defined systems or components, with scope, methodology, findings, and remediation records included.

NIST-1199.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming an independent penetration testing agent or team is employed to perform penetration testing on the system or system components.

NIST-1200.description: Provide red team exercise records confirming adversarial simulation activities were conducted in accordance with defined rules of engagement, with exercise scope, findings, and remediation outcomes documented.

NIST-1202.description: Provide internal connection authorization records confirming all defined system component connections are approved and documented with security requirements.

NIST-1203.description: Provide internal connection documentation confirming each authorized internal connection includes all required elements: (1) interface characteristics; (2) security requirements; (3) privacy requirements; and (4) the nature of the information communicated across the connection.

NIST-1204.description: Provide the internal connection configuration confirming connections are automatically terminated after CSP-defined conditions, with evidence of enforcement.

NIST-1205.description: Provide records confirming the continued need for each internal connection is reviewed at least annually, with the review date, reviewer identity, and whether each connection was retained, modified, or terminated documented.

NIST-1206.description: Provide the current baseline configuration confirming it is documented, maintained under configuration control, and reflects the current production state.

NIST-1207.description: Provide system configuration records confirming the baseline configuration is reviewed and updated: at least annually and whenever a significant change occurs; when required due to defined circumstances; and when system components are installed or upgraded — with the review date, triggering condition, and any resulting changes documented for each update.

NIST-1208.description: Provide automated configuration management tool output confirming the currency, completeness, accuracy, and availability of the baseline configuration is maintained through automated mechanisms — not manual processes alone. Include evidence that the automated tool actively enforces and tracks the baseline state.

NIST-1209.description: Provide version control records confirming previous baseline configurations are retained to support rollback capabilities.

NIST-1210.description: Provide records confirming the organization's defined approach for high-risk travel is implemented in both directions: (1) systems or components issued to individuals traveling to locations the organization deems high-risk have hardened configurations applied before departure — such as restricted-capability builds, reduced connectivity, or limited software installs — with issuance documented per individual; and (2) upon return, defined security controls are applied to those same systems or components before reconnection to the organizational network — such as malware scans, configuration integrity checks, or credential resets — with completion documented per return event.

NIST-1211.description: Provide the configuration management records documenting the types of changes that are configuration-controlled, with evidence these are enforced through the change control process.

NIST-1212.description: Provide change review and approval records confirming proposed configuration-controlled changes are reviewed and formally approved or disapproved with explicit consideration of security and privacy impact analyses — with records showing each proposed change includes a documented impact assessment before a final approval or rejection decision is made.

NIST-1213.description: Provide configuration change management records confirming all configuration change decisions associated with the system are documented — including the change description, security impact analysis, approval or denial decision, approving authority, and final disposition.

NIST-1214.description: Provide change implementation records confirming approved configuration-controlled changes are implemented as authorized, with change ticket evidence from the current period.

NIST-1215.description: Provide change management retention records confirming all configuration-controlled change records are retained for the defined period and current records are available for review.

NIST-1216.description: Provide system configuration export confirming activities associated with configuration-controlled changes to the system are monitored and reviewed.

NIST-1217.description: Provide configuration change records from the current period confirming changes were reviewed, approved, and implemented per policy.

NIST-1218.description: Provide configuration control board meeting records confirming the CCB or equivalent governance body convenes at the defined frequency, with meeting minutes or attendance records as evidence of active oversight.

NIST-1219.description: Provide the automated change management configuration confirming: (1) proposed changes to the system are documented using automated mechanisms; (2) approval authorities are automatically notified of proposed changes and change approval is requested; (3) proposed changes that have not been approved or disapproved within the agreed-upon time period are automatically highlighted; (4) automated mechanisms prevent changes until designated approvals are received; (5) all changes to the system are automatically documented; and (6) approval authorities are automatically notified when approved changes are completed — with configuration evidence confirming all six automation capabilities are active.

NIST-1220.description: Provide configuration management records confirming changes to the system are tested, validated and documented before finalizing the implementation of the changes.

NIST-1221.description: Provide configuration control board (CCB) or equivalent governance records confirming designated security and privacy representatives are required members, with meeting records or sign-off history demonstrating active participation.

NIST-1223.description: Provide the cryptographic configuration management records confirming all cryptographic mechanisms — including all security safeguards that rely on cryptography — are placed under configuration management, with the configuration baseline documentation identifying cryptographic components and the change control process applied to them.

NIST-1226.description: Provide configuration management records confirming changes to the system are analyzed to determine potential security and privacy impacts prior to change implementation.

NIST-1227.description: Provide records confirming changes to the system are analyzed for security and privacy impacts — including analysis for flaws, weaknesses, incompatibility, and intentional malice — in a separate test environment before implementation in the operational environment. Evidence must confirm: (1) a dedicated test or staging environment exists that is physically or logically separated from production — with network diagrams, access control records, or infrastructure configuration confirming isolation; (2) proposed changes are introduced and tested in that environment before any production deployment; and (3) impact analysis records documenting the security and privacy review of each change are produced and retained.

NIST-1228.description: Provide configuration management records confirming the impacted controls are implemented correctly, operating as intended and producing the desired outcome with regard to meeting the security and privacy requirements for the system after system change.

NIST-1229.description: Provide configuration management records confirming physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are defined, documented, approved and enforced.

NIST-1230.description: Provide the automated change access restriction configuration confirming only authorized personnel can implement configuration changes, with enforcement evidence.

NIST-1231.description: Provide audit configuration records and a sample audit trail confirming change enforcement actions are automatically captured in the audit log, enabling reconstruction of who made what change and when.

NIST-1232.description: Provide the production environment access configuration confirming privileges to change system components and related information are limited to authorized personnel.

NIST-1233.description: Provide privilege review records confirming that production and operational environment privileges are reviewed and reevaluated at least quarterly per FedRAMP requirements, with each review documenting the date, reviewer identity, and any privilege changes resulting from the evaluation.

NIST-1235.description: Provide SCAP-validated compliance scan results confirming: (1) scans cover every component within the authorization boundary — not a partial subset; (2) settings are benchmarked against DoD STIGs, or CIS Level 2 where STIGs are unavailable; and (3) all failed compliance checks are individually identified and documented with the specific component, check ID, and current value.

NIST-1236.description: Provide records confirming any deviations from the established configuration baseline are identified, documented, and formally approved based on operational requirements, with each deviation including a justification and authorizing official sign-off.

NIST-1237.description: Provide system configuration export confirming changes to the configuration settings are monitored and controlled in accordance with organizational policies and procedures.

NIST-1238.description: Provide the automated configuration management tool output confirming security settings for all defined system components are centrally managed, applied, and verified through automated mechanisms — not manual processes alone.

NIST-1239.description: Provide the unauthorized change response configuration confirming defined automated or manual actions are taken in response to unauthorized changes to defined configuration settings — with the response procedure, the list of monitored settings, and a sample response record from the current period.

NIST-1240.description: Provide the system configuration confirming it is configured to provide only the capabilities required for defined mission-essential functions, with unnecessary services, features, and functions disabled.

NIST-1241.description: Provide the system configuration confirming prohibited or restricted functions, ports, protocols, software, and services are disabled or blocked across all in-scope systems.

NIST-1242.description: Provide configuration management records confirming: (1) the system is reviewed at least annually to identify unnecessary or non-secure functions, ports, protocols, software, and services — with the review date, scope, and findings documented; and (2) all items identified as unnecessary or non-secure are disabled, removed, or restricted — with configuration evidence confirming hardening actions were implemented.

NIST-1243.description: Provide the application execution control configuration confirming unauthorized program execution is prevented through an allowlist, authorization rules, or rules of behavior-based control, with current enforcement evidence.

NIST-1245.description: Provide the authorized software list confirming all programs permitted to execute on the system are identified and enforced.

NIST-1246.description: Provide the application allowlisting enforcement configuration confirming a deny-all, permit-by-exception policy is implemented — only explicitly authorized software programs may execute on the system, with unauthorized execution technically blocked through enforced allowlisting.

NIST-1247.description: Provide the authorized software list review records confirming the list is reviewed and updated at least quarterly — or whenever a change occurs — with the review date, reviewer identity, and any additions or removals documented.

NIST-1250.description: Provide the system component inventory confirming all required attributes are captured and the inventory reflects the current production environment — and that it is reviewed and updated at least monthly and whenever components are installed, removed, or changed, with the most recent update date and a sample of recent change-triggered updates as evidence.

NIST-1251.description: Provide the automated inventory maintenance configuration confirming automated mechanisms are used to maintain the currency, completeness, accuracy, and availability of the system component inventory — with configuration evidence identifying the automated mechanisms and a current inventory extract.

NIST-1252.description: Provide the automated discovery configuration and response configuration confirming: (1) unauthorized hardware, software, and firmware is detected continuously or at defined intervals across all in-scope systems; and (2) when unauthorized components are detected, the system automatically takes defined response actions — such as disabling network access, isolating the component, or notifying defined personnel — with a sample detection event and its corresponding response from the current period.

NIST-1253.description: Provide the system component inventory confirming individuals responsible and accountable for administering each system component are identified by position and role — with the current inventory showing administrator accountability assignments for all in-scope components.

NIST-1254.description: Provide software usage compliance records confirming all three dimensions of software usage control are enforced: (1) software and associated documentation are used in accordance with contract agreements and applicable copyright laws — with license compliance records or legal review evidence; (2) the number of software copies in active use is monitored against licensed quantities to prevent unauthorized copying or distribution — with current license inventory and usage tracking records; and (3) peer-to-peer file sharing technologies are controlled and not used for unauthorized distribution of copyrighted material — with the P2P prohibition or control configuration confirming enforcement.

NIST-1256.description: Provide user software installation records confirming policy, enforcement, and monitoring are all in place: (1) governing rules for user-installed software are established and communicated to users — with the current software installation policy and evidence of distribution or user acknowledgment; (2) installation policies are enforced through technical controls — such as application allowlisting, privilege restrictions, or endpoint management configuration — confirming enforcement is not reliant on policy awareness alone; and (3) adherence to user software installation policies is monitored continuously through automated enforcement, with a compliance record from the current period confirming active monitoring.

NIST-1258.description: Provide the information location documentation confirming where defined information types are processed and stored within the system.

NIST-1259.description: Provide the information location documentation confirming the specific system components on which defined information types are processed and stored are identified and documented, with the inventory current and accessible.

NIST-1260.description: Provide records confirming the users with access to system components where defined information types are processed and stored are identified, documented, and subject to appropriate access controls.

NIST-1261.description: Provide records confirming any changes to the location where defined information types are processed, stored, or transmitted — including CSP-defined information such as federal customer data and other sensitive system data — are tracked and documented, with notification to relevant personnel when location changes occur for any covered information type.

NIST-1262.description: Provide automated data discovery tool output confirming federal customer data and system data requiring High or Moderate impact protection are identified by information type across defined system components, per FedRAMP requirements.

NIST-1263.description: Provide the software installation verification configuration confirming installation of defined software and firmware components is prevented unless digitally signed using a certificate recognized and approved by the organization — with configuration evidence and, where digital signatures or certificates are unavailable, documentation of alternative cryptographic integrity checks (hashes, self-signed certificates, etc.) used as compensating controls.

NIST-1264.description: Provide records confirming contingency planning activities are coordinated with incident handling activities — such as shared personnel, joint exercises, or documented coordination procedures between the CP and IR teams.

NIST-1265.description: Provide the capacity planning documentation confirming necessary capacity exists during contingency operations for information processing, telecommunications, and environmental support — with capacity assessments, resource projections, and evidence that contingency capacity requirements are actively planned and provisioned.

NIST-1266.description: Provide the continuity planning documentation confirming: (1) continuance of essential mission and business functions is planned for in the event of system disruption; and (2) continuity is sustained until full system restoration at primary processing and/or storage sites — with the contingency plan sections addressing both elements.

NIST-1267.description: Provide records confirming critical system assets supporting essential mission and business functions are identified, with the identification informing contingency plan priorities.

NIST-1268.description: Provide contingency training completion records confirming all personnel completed required training. Privileged administrators and engineers must complete basic contingency training within 10 days of assignment per FedRAMP requirements, with critical system training completed before accessing those systems.

NIST-1269.description: Provide contingency training records confirming training is provided to applicable personnel when system changes require it — with training completion dates corresponding to the specific change that triggered the training requirement.

NIST-1270.description: Provide contingency training completion records confirming applicable personnel receive recurring contingency training at least annually, with training completion dates and participant records from the current cycle.

NIST-1271.description: Provide contingency training records confirming simulated events — such as tabletop exercises, drills, or functional simulations — are incorporated into contingency training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations, with training scenario documentation and participant completion records.

NIST-1272.description: Provide the contingency plan test records confirming functional exercises — not tabletop-only reviews — are used to validate plan effectiveness and organizational readiness to execute the plan.

NIST-1273.description: Provide corrective action records confirming any deficiencies identified during contingency plan testing have corresponding remediation plans with assigned owners and target completion dates.

NIST-1274.description: Provide contingency plan test records confirming the plan is tested at the alternate processing site to: (1) familiarize contingency personnel with the facility and available resources; and (2) evaluate the capabilities of the alternate processing site to support contingency operations — with test results, identified gaps, and corrective actions.

NIST-1275.description: Provide the alternate storage site agreement or configuration confirming a geographically separate storage site is established, actively used, and sufficiently separated from the primary site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats — with evidence of the physical separation and separation rationale documented.

NIST-1276.description: Provide contingency records confirming establishment of the alternate storage site includes necessary agreements to permit the storage and retrieval of system backup information.

NIST-1277.description: Provide evidence confirming security controls at the alternate site are equivalent to those at the primary site.

NIST-1278.description: Provide the alternate storage site configuration records confirming the site is configured to facilitate recovery operations in accordance with the recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) defined in the service provider and organization SLA — with site configuration documentation and RTO/RPO alignment evidence.

NIST-1279.description: Provide the alternate storage site risk assessment and mitigation plan confirming: (1) potential accessibility problems to the site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster are identified; and (2) explicit mitigation actions to address each identified problem are outlined — with both the risk identification and the corresponding mitigations documented.

NIST-1280.description: Provide evidence confirming the alternate processing site can resume operations within the defined recovery time objective. Include: (1) a description of how responsibilities are divided between the CSP and any underlying cloud or hosting provider for alternate processing; (2) the specific failover configuration in place — not just that an alternate site exists; and (3) if this control is inherited from an infrastructure provider, evidence confirming the provider's Customer Responsibility Matrix (CRM) has been reviewed and tested for this control.

NIST-1281.description: Provide records confirming equipment and supplies are available at the alternate processing site. Include a description of the CSP's failover configuration and a clear delineation of which responsibilities are owned by the CSP versus any underlying infrastructure provider.

NIST-1282.description: Provide alternate site activation records or test results confirming the system can resume essential functions at the alternate site within the required timeframe. Include the responsibility delineation between the CSP and any infrastructure provider, and — if inherited — evidence the provider's CRM was reviewed and tested.

NIST-1283.description: Provide evidence confirming security controls at the alternate processing site are equivalent to those at the primary site, including the CSP-versus-provider responsibility breakdown and documentation that equivalency has been validated.

NIST-1284.description: Provide contingency records confirming an alternate processing site that is sufficiently separated from the primary processing site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats is identified.

NIST-1285.description: Provide the alternate processing site risk assessment and mitigation plan confirming: (1) potential accessibility problems to the site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster are identified; and (2) explicit mitigation actions to address each identified problem are outlined — with both the risk identification and the corresponding mitigations documented.

NIST-1286.description: Provide the alternate processing site agreements confirming they contain priority-of-service provisions consistent with the system's availability requirements and recovery time objectives.

NIST-1287.description: Provide alternate processing site preparation records confirming the site is prepared and ready to serve as the operational site supporting essential mission and business functions — with readiness assessments, equipment staging records, and evidence the site can assume operational status within required timeframes.

NIST-1288.description: Provide the alternate telecommunications service agreements confirming services are established with the necessary provisions to support resumption of essential system operations within the defined timeframe.

NIST-1289.description: Provide telecommunications service documentation confirming: (1) primary and alternate service agreements both contain priority-of-service provisions consistent with the system's availability requirements; and (2) Telecommunications Service Priority (TSP) authorization is established for all applicable telecommunications services used — with current TSP authorization documentation and service agreement extracts as evidence.

NIST-1290.description: Provide alternate telecommunications service agreements confirming providers and paths are separate from primary services to eliminate shared points of failure.

NIST-1291.description: Provide telecommunications service records confirming alternate providers are geographically and infrastructure-separated from primary service providers — reducing susceptibility to the same threats — with provider documentation confirming physical and logical separation between primary and alternate telecommunications paths.
