NIST-1292.description: Provide telecommunications provider contingency documentation confirming: (1) primary and alternate providers are contractually required to have contingency plans; (2) provider contingency plans have been reviewed to confirm they meet organizational requirements; and (3) evidence of contingency testing and training by providers is obtained at least annually — with plan review records and annual test/training evidence.

NIST-1293.description: Provide backup execution logs confirming user-level data is backed up daily (incremental) and weekly (full) with at least three copies maintained — at least one available online — per FedRAMP requirements. Include a description of how backup responsibilities are divided between the CSP and any underlying infrastructure provider. If backups are managed by the provider, include evidence the provider's CRM was reviewed and tested for this control.

NIST-1294.description: Provide backup execution logs confirming system-level data is backed up at the required frequency with at least three copies maintained, at least one available online. Include the CSP-versus-provider responsibility delineation and, if inherited, evidence the provider's CRM was reviewed and tested.

NIST-1295.description: Provide backup execution records confirming system documentation — including security documentation — is backed up at the required frequency with at least three copies, at least one available online. Include the responsibility delineation and, if inherited, evidence the provider's CRM was reviewed and tested.

NIST-1296.description: Provide contingency records confirming the confidentiality, integrity and availability of backup information is protected.

NIST-1297.description: Provide backup configuration and execution records confirming user-level information, system-level information, and system documentation are backed up at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements. Include current backup schedule configuration, execution logs, and backup verification records confirming the backup schedule and integrity checks are enforced.

NIST-1298.description: Provide contingency test records confirming a sample of backup information is used to test the restoration of selected system functions as part of contingency plan testing — with restoration test results and evidence of successful functional recovery from backup.

NIST-1299.description: Provide the backup storage configuration confirming backup copies of defined critical system software and other security-related information are stored in a separate facility or in a fire-rated container not co-located with the operational system — with storage location documentation and separation evidence.

NIST-1300.description: Provide the backup transfer configuration confirming system backup information is transferred to the alternate storage site: (1) within a time period consistent with the RTO and RPO defined in the service provider and organization SLA; and (2) at a transfer rate consistent with those same objectives — with transfer logs and RTO/RPO alignment documentation.

NIST-1301.description: Provide contingency test or activation records confirming the system can be recovered and reconstituted to a known state within the defined RTO and RPO, with actual recovery completion time documented.

NIST-1302.description: Provide contingency records confirming transaction recovery is implemented for systems that are transaction-based.

NIST-1303.description: Provide the system restoration configuration confirming the capability exists to restore system components within the restoration time period defined in the service provider and organization SLA — from configuration-controlled and integrity-protected backups representing a known, operational state — with restoration procedure documentation and a sample test result.

NIST-1304.description: Provide the user account configuration and authentication records confirming all organizational users are uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing the system. Each user must have a distinct account — no shared or generic accounts permitted — with unique credentials tied to that identity.

NIST-1305.description: Provide the system configuration confirming that processes acting on behalf of authenticated users are associated with and bound to that user's unique identity — for example, through inherited session tokens, service account mapping, or process ownership records — so that actions taken by automated processes are attributable to the originating user.

NIST-1306.description: Provide MFA configuration and test evidence for EVERY access point into the system where privileged accounts authenticate — including VPN, cloud console, web portal, SSH, RDP, and any other access path. For each access point: (1) identify the specific MFA solution by product name — not a generic 'MFA is in place'; (2) confirm a privileged user's authentication was tested; and (3) confirm whether the solution is phishing-resistant (FIDO2/WebAuthn) or phishing-susceptible (TOTP/push notification).

NIST-1307.description: Provide MFA configuration and test evidence for EVERY access point into the system where non-privileged accounts authenticate. This must be independently validated — not copied from the IA-2(1) write-up unless there are genuinely no non-privileged users. Identify the specific MFA solution by product name for each access path and confirm whether it is phishing-resistant.

NIST-1308.description: Provide the shared account authentication configuration confirming individual authentication is required before granting access to any shared account or resource.

NIST-1309.description: Provide MFA configuration and device evidence confirming the MFA token or push notification appears on a SEPARATE physical device from the one used to access the system. Identify the specific MFA solution by product name and provide the FIPS 140-2 validation certificate confirming the solution is validated.

NIST-1310.description: Provide the authentication mechanism configuration confirming replay-resistant mechanisms — such as challenge-response or one-time tokens — are implemented for both privileged and non-privileged account access.

NIST-1311.description: Provide evidence confirming the system accepts PIV/CAC credentials — this control must be marked as implemented, not N/A, even if the CSP does not use PIV for its own workforce. Clearly identify the federation mechanism in place that allows federal agency users to authenticate using their agency-issued PIV/CAC — such as a SAML assertion, OIDC integration, or an identity federation service.

NIST-1312.description: Provide the device identification and authentication configuration confirming all defined device types are uniquely identified and authenticated before establishing local, network, or remote connections to the system.

NIST-1314.description: Provide identifier management records confirming the full identifier lifecycle is controlled: (1) identifier assignments are authorized by the ISSO or equivalent role before being issued to individuals, groups, roles, services, or devices; (2) each identifier uniquely and unambiguously identifies its assigned party — no ambiguous, shared, or generic identifiers permitted; (3) each identifier is formally assigned to the intended individual, group, role, service, or device before it is used to access the system; and (4) deactivated identifiers are not reused for at least two (2) years following deactivation, per FedRAMP requirements — with the identifier management configuration, assignment authorization records, and identifier reuse policy all available as evidence.

NIST-1315.description: Provide identifier management records confirming contractors and foreign nationals are uniquely identified as such — with their affiliation reflected in the identifier or access record — not grouped under generic accounts.

NIST-1317.description: Provide the authenticator management configuration confirming all authenticators comply with NIST SP 800-63-3 at IAL, AAL, and FAL Level 2 or higher. Include records showing how user identity is verified before authenticators are issued — specify the service or process by name (e.g., the identity proofing provider used and the method applied) rather than a generic statement. Reference I-9 employment authorization forms or equivalent identity verification artifacts.

NIST-1318.description: Provide the authenticator configuration confirming default or initial authenticator content is changed upon first use.

NIST-1319.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming system authenticators are managed to ensure that authenticators have sufficient strength of mechanism for their intended use.

NIST-1320.description: Provide the authenticator lifecycle management records confirming procedures are in place and followed for: initial distribution, handling of lost or compromised authenticators, revoking authenticators for departing users, and recovering from authenticator compromise.

NIST-1321.description: Provide records confirming all default system and application authenticators — including default passwords, certificates, and shared secrets — are changed before first use.

NIST-1322.description: Provide the authenticator refresh configuration confirming authenticators are changed or refreshed within the defined period for each authenticator type and immediately upon defined triggering events such as suspected compromise or personnel role change.

NIST-1323.description: Provide the authenticator protection configuration confirming authenticator content — such as password hashes, private keys, or token seeds — is protected from unauthorized disclosure and modification at rest and in transit.

NIST-1324.description: Provide records confirming individuals are required to take specific protective actions for their authenticators — such as not sharing credentials, securing hardware tokens, and reporting suspected compromise — with evidence these requirements are communicated, acknowledged, and enforced.

NIST-1325.description: Provide the device configuration confirming devices implement specific technical controls to protect authenticators — such as encrypted storage of credentials, secure key storage, or equivalent device-level protections — with configuration evidence across all applicable device types.

NIST-1326.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming system authenticators are managed through the change of authenticators for group or role accounts when membership to those accounts changes.

NIST-1327.description: Provide the password configuration confirming it complies with NIST SP 800-63B: passwords are checked against commonly used or compromised password lists, minimum length is enforced, and the configuration does not impose periodic forced changes, complexity rules, or hints — per FedRAMP's SP 800-63B alignment requirement.

NIST-1328.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming for password-based authentication when passwords are created or updated by users, the passwords are verified not to be found on the list of commonly used, expected, or compromised passwords in IA-05(0.

NIST-1329.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming for password-based authentication, immediate selection of a new password is required upon account recovery.

NIST-1330.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming for password-based authentication, user selection of long passwords and passphrases is allowed, including spaces and all printable characters.

NIST-1331.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming for password-based authentication, automated tools are employed to assist the user in selecting strong password authenticators.

NIST-1332.description: Provide the password composition and complexity configuration confirming all enforced rules meet or exceed CSP-defined requirements across all in-scope authentication systems.

NIST-1333.description: Provide the PKI and public key-based authentication configuration confirming: (1) access to private keys is restricted to authorized individuals or processes only — with access controls, key storage configuration, and audit records confirming no unauthorized access; (2) the authenticated identity established by the certificate is mapped to the corresponding individual or group account — no certificate is accepted for authentication unless a valid account mapping exists; (3) only approved trust anchors are used — NSA-approved, PKI Class 3, or PKI Class 4 — with the trust anchor configuration confirming no unauthorized CAs are trusted; and (4) certificate revocation checks are enforced and certificate status is validated before use — with CRL or OCSP configuration confirming revocation is checked in real time or near-real time prior to authentication.

NIST-1334.description: Provide the authenticator protection configuration confirming authenticators are protected at a level commensurate with the security category of the information to which they grant access — higher-sensitivity systems require stronger authenticator protections.

NIST-1335.description: Provide configuration records and code review evidence confirming unencrypted static authenticators — such as hardcoded passwords, API keys, or tokens — are not embedded in applications, scripts, configuration files, or any other form of static storage.

NIST-1336.description: Provide the multi-system authenticator management configuration confirming security controls are implemented to manage the risk of compromise due to individuals having accounts on multiple systems — specifically requiring different authenticators in different user authentication domains — with configuration evidence confirming authenticator uniqueness across system boundaries (unless a documented and controlled single-sign-on architecture is in place).

NIST-1337.description: Provide the cached authenticator configuration confirming use of cached authenticators is prohibited after the defined time period — consistent with applicable configuration baselines such as STIG or CIS standards — with configuration output confirming cache expiration is enforced and cached credentials cannot be used beyond the defined window.

NIST-1340.description: Provide a screenshot or system demonstration confirming authentication feedback — such as passwords — is obscured during the authentication process.

NIST-1341.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming mechanisms for authentication to a cryptographic module are implemented that meet the requirements of applicable laws, executive orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidelines fo.

NIST-1342.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming non-organizational users or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users are uniquely identified and authenticated.

NIST-1343.description: Provide evidence confirming the system accepts PIV/CAC credentials from federal agency users — this control must be marked as implemented, not N/A. Clearly identify: (1) the federation mechanism enabling federal agency authentication, such as a SAML assertion, OIDC integration, or equivalent identity federation service; and (2) confirmation that the capability to accept PIV/CAC from additional agencies is already in place.

NIST-1344.description: Provide the external authenticator acceptance configuration confirming only NIST SP 800-63-compliant external authenticators are accepted.

NIST-1345.description: Provide the documented and maintained list of accepted external authenticators confirming which external identity providers and authenticator types have been reviewed and approved for use — with the list current, version-controlled, and accessible to relevant personnel.

NIST-1346.description: Provide records confirming the system conforms with defined identity management profiles — such as FICAM-approved profiles or equivalent federal identity standards — with conformance evidence available.

NIST-1349.description: Provide the re-authentication configuration confirming session time limits do not exceed NIST SP 800-63B AAL3 thresholds — the applicable level for DoD IL5: no more than 12 hours of absolute session duration and no more than 15 minutes of inactivity before re-authentication is required. Include a log record or active session policy output confirming these limits are enforced across all in-scope authentication points.

NIST-1350.description: Provide identity assurance records confirming users requiring logical access are identity-proofed at the appropriate assurance level as specified in NIST SP 800-63A and applicable FedRAMP guidelines before accounts are issued.

NIST-1351.description: Provide records confirming each user identity is resolved to a unique individual — with no shared identities between different people — and that this resolution is documented in the registration process.

NIST-1352.description: Provide records confirming identity evidence is collected, validated for authenticity, and verified against the presenting individual as part of the enrollment process.

NIST-1354.description: Provide identity proofing records confirming individuals presented valid identity evidence to the registration authority before accounts or credentials were issued.

NIST-1355.description: Provide identity proofing records confirming presented identity evidence was validated and verified using defined methods — confirming the evidence is genuine, current, and belongs to the presenting individual.

NIST-1356.description: Provide records confirming validation and verification of identity evidence for user enrollment is conducted in-person before a designated registration authority — with the identity proofing process documentation, registration authority designation, and records confirming in-person verification for applicable accounts.

NIST-1357.description: Provide identity proofing records confirming a registration code or notice of proofing is delivered through an out-of-band channel — separate from the primary registration channel — to verify the user's physical or digital address of record.

NIST-1358.description: Provide incident response training completion records confirming personnel in IR roles completed training within FedRAMP-required timeframes — within 10 days for privileged users and within 30 days for those assuming IR roles — prior to participating in IR activities.

NIST-1359.description: Provide records confirming incident response training is provided when system changes require it, with training completion dates corresponding to the change that triggered the training requirement.

NIST-1360.description: Provide incident response training completion records confirming all applicable personnel receive recurring training at least annually.

NIST-1361.description: Provide IR training content review records confirming training materials are reviewed and updated at least annually to reflect the current threat environment, lessons learned, and regulatory changes — and also following significant events such as an actual incident, a major system change, or an after-action review finding — with the review date, triggering condition, and any resulting content changes documented for each review.

NIST-1362.description: Provide incident response training records confirming simulated events — such as tabletop exercises, drills, or functional simulations — are incorporated into training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations, with scenario documentation, participant records, and evaluation results.

NIST-1363.description: Provide records confirming an automated incident response training environment is provided using defined automated mechanisms — with configuration evidence identifying the training platform and records confirming its use for IR training delivery.

NIST-1364.description: Provide incident response test records confirming the IR capability is tested at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, including functional exercises where required. Include test plans, results, findings, and evidence that testing was conducted at the required frequency and any identified improvements were addressed.

NIST-1366.description: Provide incident records from the current period confirming the IR capability addresses all five phases: preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery. Each phase must have supporting evidence — a single ticket with a one-line description of each phase is insufficient.

NIST-1367.description: Provide records confirming incident handling activities are coordinated with contingency planning activities — for example, that incident-triggered continuity actions are documented and that IR and CP personnel communicate during and after significant incidents.

NIST-1368.description: Provide records confirming lessons learned from incident handling activities are documented and incorporated into updated IR procedures, training content, and future testing scenarios.

NIST-1369.description: Provide incident records confirming the changes resulting from the incorporated lessons learned are implemented accordingly.

NIST-1370.description: Provide incident records confirming the rigor and scope of investigations are commensurate with the threat level of the incident.

NIST-1371.description: Provide the automated incident handling mechanism configuration and a sample automated action from the current period confirming the system supports incident handling through automated mechanisms — such as automated ticketing, alerting, or escalation.

NIST-1372.description: Provide the dynamic reconfiguration capability configuration confirming the incident response capability includes the ability to dynamically reconfigure defined system components — across all network, data storage, and computing devices — as part of incident response, with IR procedures and configuration evidence confirming dynamic reconfiguration is an active component of the IR plan.

NIST-1374.description: Provide records confirming individual incident information and incident responses are correlated across the organization to achieve an organization-wide perspective on incident awareness and response — with evidence of cross-incident correlation, shared situational awareness processes, and any resulting coordinated actions.

NIST-1375.description: Provide the insider threat incident handling documentation confirming an incident handling capability is established specifically for incidents involving insider threats — with insider threat IR procedures, detection criteria, escalation paths, and any insider threat incidents handled during the review period.

NIST-1379.description: Provide the integrated incident response team documentation confirming: (1) an integrated incident response team is established and maintained; and (2) the team can be deployed to any location identified by the organization within the defined time period — with team composition records, deployment procedures, and readiness evidence.

NIST-1383.description: Provide incident tracking records from the current period confirming all reported incidents are logged in the incident tracking system with consistent documentation — including detection date, status, category, and resolution.

NIST-1384.description: Provide the automated incident tracking configuration confirming incidents are tracked, collected, and analyzed using defined automated mechanisms — with configuration evidence identifying the automated incident management platform and records confirming its operational use during the review period.

NIST-1385.description: Provide incident reporting records confirming: (1) personnel report suspected incidents to the organizational incident response capability within US-CERT reporting timelines; and (2) incident information is reported to all defined authorities — including US-CERT and CISA — with timestamps, report copies, and evidence of timely external notification.

NIST-1386.description: Provide the automated incident reporting mechanism configuration and a sample report confirming incidents are reported to defined authorities using automated mechanisms.

NIST-1388.description: Provide records confirming incident information is shared with the product or service provider and other entities in the supply chain when an incident involves a system component or service from the supply chain.

NIST-1389.description: Provide records confirming an incident response support resource is available to system users and actively provides practical advice and assistance for handling and reporting incidents — with contact information, availability records, and examples of support provided from the current period.

NIST-1390.description: Provide the automated IR support mechanism configuration confirming automated tools increase the availability of IR information and support — such as automated alert routing, IR knowledge base, or automated ticketing — with evidence the mechanism is active.

NIST-1392.description: Provide the Incident Response Plan confirming it is developed and addresses all required content elements: (1) a roadmap for implementing the incident response capability; (2) the structure and organization of the IR capability; (3) a high-level approach for how the IR capability fits into the overall organization; (4) unique requirements of the organization with regard to mission, size, structure, and functions; (5) a definition of reportable incidents consistent with the FISMA definition; (6) metrics for measuring the IR capability within the organization; (7) the resources and management support needed to effectively maintain and mature the IR capability; (8) how incident information will be shared; (9) evidence the plan is reviewed and approved by defined personnel at least annually; and (10) explicit designation of responsibility for incident response to defined entities, personnel, or roles.

NIST-1393.description: Provide records confirming specific personnel or roles are formally assigned responsibility for responding to information spills, with designation documented and current.

NIST-1394.description: Provide incident records confirming the specific information involved in the system contamination is identified in response to information spills.

NIST-1395.description: Provide information spillage response records confirming designated personnel are alerted via a communication method not associated with the spilled information — ensuring the notification channel itself is not compromised.

NIST-1396.description: Provide incident records confirming the contaminated system or system component is isolated in response to information spills.

NIST-1397.description: Provide incident records confirming the information is eradicated from the contaminated system or component in response to information spills.

NIST-1398.description: Provide incident records confirming other systems or system components that may have been subsequently contaminated are identified in response to information spills.

NIST-1399.description: Provide records from the most recent information spillage response confirming all defined response actions — notification, containment, analysis, and eradication — were completed with dates and responsible parties documented.

NIST-1400.description: Provide the information spillage procedures and supporting records confirming personnel impacted by information spills can continue their assigned tasks while affected systems are undergoing corrective action.

NIST-1401.description: Provide records confirming defined controls are applied to personnel exposed to information outside their authorized access — such as security debriefs, access restriction records, or formal notification and remediation procedures.

NIST-1402.description: Provide maintenance records confirming scheduled maintenance is documented and carried out in accordance with manufacturer or vendor specifications, with work orders or service tickets showing planned dates, completed dates, and activities performed.

NIST-1403.description: Provide maintenance approval and monitoring records confirming all activities — whether performed on-site, remotely, or at an alternate location — are approved prior to execution and monitored throughout, with technician identification and completion sign-off.

NIST-1404.description: Provide removal approval records confirming designated personnel or roles explicitly approve the removal of system components from organizational facilities prior to off-site maintenance, repair, or replacement, with approval dates and component details documented per activity.

NIST-1405.description: Provide equipment sanitization records confirming system media is sanitized to remove all defined organizational information before components are removed from organizational facilities for off-site maintenance, repair, or replacement, with sanitization method and completion date documented per activity.

NIST-1406.description: Provide post-maintenance verification records confirming that potentially impacted security controls were checked and confirmed operational following maintenance, repair, or replacement activities.

NIST-1407.description: Provide maintenance records confirming all required organizational information is captured in each maintenance record — including date, description of work, personnel involved, and any relevant security-relevant findings from the activity.

NIST-1408.description: Provide the automated maintenance management configuration confirming: (1) defined automated mechanisms are used to schedule, conduct, and document maintenance, repair, and replacement actions; and (2) up-to-date, accurate, and complete records of all maintenance, repair, and replacement actions — requested, scheduled, in-progress, and completed — are produced — with configuration evidence and a current maintenance record extract.

NIST-1409.description: Provide maintenance tool management records confirming: (1) tools brought into the facility for use during maintenance activities are on an approved list, with their use controlled and monitored; and (2) previously approved tools are reviewed at the defined frequency to verify they remain appropriate for use — with the approved tool list, current monitoring records, and the most recent tool review as evidence.

NIST-1410.description: Provide maintenance tool inspection records confirming tools are checked for improper or unauthorized modifications before being used on organizational systems, with documented inspection results.

NIST-1411.description: Provide records confirming diagnostic and test media used for maintenance are scanned for malicious code before use on organizational systems, with scan completion documented per maintenance activity.

NIST-1412.description: Provide records confirming maintenance equipment is either verified to contain no organizational data before removal, or sanitized before removal, with the information owner's authorization documented for any equipment leaving the facility.

NIST-1416.description: Provide remote maintenance session authorization and monitoring records confirming all nonlocal maintenance activities are approved prior to initiation and actively monitored throughout the session.

NIST-1417.description: Provide the remote maintenance tool configuration confirming only tools consistent with organizational policy are permitted for nonlocal maintenance, with records showing tool approvals are current.

NIST-1418.description: Provide the authentication configuration for remote maintenance sessions confirming strong — multi-factor — authentication is required to establish any nonlocal maintenance or diagnostic session.

NIST-1419.description: Provide maintenance records confirming nonlocal sessions are logged with session details — initiation time, termination time, actions performed, and personnel involved — available for review.

NIST-1420.description: Provide the configuration confirming nonlocal maintenance sessions are automatically terminated when they are no longer active, preventing unattended open maintenance connections.

NIST-1422.description: Provide nonlocal maintenance security records confirming: (1) nonlocal maintenance or diagnostic services are performed from a system with a security capability comparable to the system being serviced; or (2) when this is not feasible, the component to be serviced is removed from the system prior to service, sanitized of organizational data, and then inspected and sanitized for potentially malicious software after service before reconnection — with maintenance records, sanitization records, and inspection documentation.

NIST-1426.description: Provide the maintenance personnel authorization list and process documentation confirming only authorized organizations or individuals are permitted to perform maintenance, with the current authorized list maintained and available.

NIST-1427.description: Provide access authorization records confirming non-escorted maintenance personnel have the required access authorizations before being permitted to perform unaccompanied maintenance on the system.

NIST-1428.description: Provide maintenance supervision or monitoring records confirming maintenance activities are observed and documented.

NIST-1429.description: Provide records confirming that when maintenance personnel lack the required security clearances, fully cleared organizational personnel escort and supervise those individuals throughout the maintenance activity.

NIST-1430.description: Provide sanitization records confirming all volatile information storage components are sanitized before maintenance personnel without required clearances are permitted access to the system.

NIST-1431.description: Provide records confirming alternate security controls are defined and implemented when a system cannot be sanitized, removed, or disconnected before maintenance by personnel without required clearances — with the alternate controls approved and documented.

NIST-1433.description: Provide maintenance contracts or service agreements confirming timely maintenance support and spare parts availability within the defined timeframe.

NIST-1434.description: Provide the media access control records confirming access to all system media containing sensitive information is restricted to authorized personnel or roles — covering both digital media (such as removable drives and optical media) and non-digital media (such as printed documents and hardcopy records).

NIST-1435.description: Provide system media marking samples and management records confirming: (1) all applicable media is marked to indicate distribution limitations, handling caveats, and applicable security markings — with evidence the markings reflect the sensitivity of the information; and (2) any media types exempt from marking requirements remain within the controlled areas where the exemption applies.

NIST-1436.description: Provide physical media storage records confirming all system media containing sensitive information — both digital and non-digital — is physically controlled and securely stored within controlled areas, and remains protected until formally destroyed or sanitized using approved methods. Evidence must confirm controls are applied to digital media (such as removable drives and optical media), non-digital media (such as printed documents and hardcopy records), and all media types pending disposal.

NIST-1437.description: Provide media transport records confirming all media containing sensitive information is protected and controlled during transport outside controlled areas. Digital media must be encrypted using FIPS-validated or NSA-approved cryptography before leaving the secure environment (see SC-13). Non-digital media must be secured in a locked container. Evidence must confirm controls are applied before departure, not in transit.

NIST-1438.description: Provide media transport accountability records confirming chain-of-custody is maintained throughout transport — including dispatch records, receipt confirmations, and tracking documentation.

NIST-1439.description: Provide records confirming all activities associated with the transport of system media outside controlled areas are documented — including the media type, transport date, method used, individuals involved, origin, destination, and chain-of-custody information.

NIST-1440.description: Provide the authorized media transport personnel list and transport activity records confirming: (1) only designated individuals are approved to transport system media outside of controlled areas; and (2) all transport activities are restricted to those authorized individuals — with transport logs or chain-of-custody records confirming only approved personnel conducted transport during the review period.

NIST-1441.description: Provide media sanitization records confirming: (1) all defined media types are sanitized using techniques and procedures consistent with NIST SP 800-88 Section 4 prior to disposal, release from organizational control, or reuse; and (2) the sanitization mechanisms employed have strength and integrity commensurate with the security category of the information — with the method, media type, and security category documented per sanitization event.

NIST-1442.description: Provide media sanitization program records confirming: (1) sanitization and disposal actions are reviewed, approved, tracked, documented, and verified — compliant with NIST SP 800-88; (2) sanitization equipment and procedures are tested at least every six (6) months to ensure the intended sanitization is being achieved; and (3) non-destructive sanitization techniques are applied to portable storage devices prior to connecting to the system under defined circumstances — with sanitization records, equipment test results, and portable media procedures.

NIST-1443.description: Provide the media use restriction configuration confirming: (1) defined media types are restricted or prohibited on specified system components, with records of any authorized exceptions documented; and (2) portable storage devices with no identifiable owner are prohibited from connecting to organizational systems — with technical controls or policy enforcement evidence confirming both restrictions are actively enforced.

NIST-1444.description: Provide physical access records confirming a list of individuals with authorized access to the facility where the system resides has been developed, approved, and maintained.

NIST-1445.description: Provide records confirming authorization credentials — such as access badges, PINs, or access cards — are issued only to individuals on the approved facility access list, with issuance records available.

NIST-1446.description: Provide physical access list review records confirming the physical access list is reviewed and updated at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements. Include the review date, reviewer identity, and documentation of any access modifications resulting from each review.

NIST-1447.description: Provide records confirming individuals are removed from the facility access list and their credentials are revoked promptly when access is no longer required — such as upon separation or role change.

NIST-1448.description: Provide the physical access control configuration confirming individual access authorizations are verified before granting access to the facility where the system resides — at all defined entry and exit points — using approved physical access control systems, devices, or guards.

NIST-1449.description: Provide the physical access control configuration confirming ingress and egress are enforced at all defined entry and exit points using approved access control systems, devices, or guards, with access logs confirming enforcement is active.

NIST-1450.description: Provide the physical access log configuration confirming logs are maintained at all defined entry and exit points, with current log records showing timestamps and individual identification.

NIST-1451.description: Provide records confirming access to areas within the facility where publicly accessible information systems reside is controlled and monitored to prevent unauthorized physical access to those systems.

NIST-1452.description: Provide visitor escort records from the current period confirming all visitors are escorted within the facility and access is documented.

NIST-1453.description: Provide visitor control records confirming visitor activity is supervised in all circumstances within restricted access areas where the system resides — not limited to entry and exit points.

NIST-1454.description: Provide records confirming keys, combinations, and other physical access devices are managed throughout their lifecycle: inventoried with each device accounted for; stored in defined secure locations with access restricted to authorized individuals only; issued with formal issuance records tracking which devices are assigned to which individuals or roles; and managed through documented procedures confirming the full chain of custody is maintained from issuance through return or destruction.

NIST-1455.description: Provide physical access device inventory records confirming keys, combinations, and other physical access devices are inventoried at least annually.

NIST-1456.description: Provide records confirming access combinations are changed at least annually — or earlier when a security-relevant event occurs such as personnel departure or suspected compromise — with change dates and triggering conditions documented.

NIST-1457.description: Provide records confirming physical keys are changed at least annually — or earlier when keys are lost or when individuals with key access are transferred or terminated — with change dates and triggering conditions documented.

NIST-1458.description: Provide physical access control records confirming physical access authorizations to the system are enforced not only at the facility level but also at defined physical spaces containing system components — with configuration and access control evidence covering component-level physical security enforcement.

NIST-1459.description: Provide records confirming physical access to system distribution and transmission lines within the facility is controlled using defined security controls — such as locked conduits, wiring closets, or cable trays with restricted access.

NIST-1460.description: Provide the access controls configuration for output devices — such as printers and display screens — confirming physical access is restricted to prevent unauthorized individuals from viewing or retrieving output.

NIST-1461.description: Provide physical monitoring records confirming access to the facility is actively monitored and that a defined response process exists for detected physical security incidents.

NIST-1462.description: Provide physical access log review records confirming logs are reviewed at least monthly per FedRAMP requirements and also upon the occurrence of defined triggering events or potential indicators — with the review date, reviewer identity, and any findings documented for each review cycle.

NIST-1463.description: Provide coordination records confirming both physical access log review results and physical security investigation results are shared with the organizational incident response capability — with referral records, joint review documentation, or IR coordination artifacts as evidence.

NIST-1464.description: Provide the intrusion detection and surveillance system configuration confirming physical intrusion alarms and surveillance equipment are deployed at the facility, with evidence the systems are active.

NIST-1465.description: Provide physical access monitoring records confirming monitoring is extended beyond facility-level coverage to include defined physical spaces containing one or more system components — with camera coverage maps, sensor placement documentation, or equivalent evidence confirming component-level physical monitoring is active.

NIST-1466.description: Provide visitor access log records confirming the full visitor access management program is operational: (1) visitor access log entries are maintained for at least one (1) year per FedRAMP requirements — with the retention configuration and current log records available; (2) logs are reviewed at least monthly per FedRAMP requirements — with the most recent review records confirming review was performed and any anomalies addressed; and (3) visitor access anomalies identified during review are reported to defined personnel upon discovery — with a sample anomaly report or confirmation that the reporting mechanism is active.

NIST-1467.description: Provide visitor access records and review evidence confirming: (1) visitor access records are maintained using defined automated mechanisms; and (2) visitor access records are reviewed using defined automated mechanisms — with system configuration evidence and current visitor log records.

NIST-1469.description: Provide records confirming power equipment and cabling is protected from physical damage and destruction.

NIST-1470.description: Provide documentation confirming emergency power-off capability exists for all defined system components or locations, with the shutoff mechanism identified, accessible, and tested.

NIST-1471.description: Provide records confirming emergency power shutoff switches or devices are placed at defined locations — at a minimum near egress points of the IT area — and are clearly labeled, with protective measures preventing accidental activation while keeping them accessible to authorized personnel.

NIST-1472.description: Provide records confirming the emergency power shutoff capability is protected from unauthorized activation through physical controls — such as protective covers, restricted access enclosures, or equivalent safeguards — preventing accidental or malicious use.

NIST-1473.description: Provide UPS documentation and test records confirming an uninterruptible power supply is in place to support an orderly system shutdown or transition to long-term alternate power in the event of a primary power source failure.

NIST-1474.description: Provide alternate power supply records confirming: (1) the alternate power supply activates automatically upon loss of primary power; and (2) the alternate power supply can maintain minimally required operational capability during an extended loss of the primary power source — with automatic transfer switch configuration and load test records confirming both capabilities.

NIST-1475.description: Provide emergency lighting inspection records confirming automatic emergency lighting that activates in the event of a power outage or disruption is employed and maintained for the system, and that coverage extends to all emergency exits and evacuation routes within the facility.

NIST-1476.description: Provide fire protection system documentation and inspection records confirming both fire detection and fire suppression systems: (1) are employed at the facility where the system resides; (2) are supported by an independent energy source so they remain operational during a power outage or disruption; and (3) are maintained and tested in accordance with applicable standards — with current inspection and maintenance records for both systems.

NIST-1477.description: Provide physical access records confirming fire detection systems that activate automatically are employed in the event of a fire.

NIST-1478.description: Provide fire detection system records confirming automatic notification is configured to alert both building maintenance or physical security personnel and emergency responders when fire is detected.

NIST-1479.description: Provide fire suppression system inspection records confirming automatic activation capability is in place and tested within the required frequency.

NIST-1480.description: Provide fire suppression system records confirming automatic notification to defined personnel and emergency responders is configured and tested.

NIST-1481.description: Provide physical access records confirming an automatic fire suppression capability is employed when the facility is not staffed on a continuous basis.

NIST-1482.description: Provide environmental monitoring records confirming: (1) temperature, humidity, and other relevant environmental conditions are maintained within CSP-defined thresholds appropriate for the system and facility — consistent with ASHRAE standards for the equipment type where applicable; and (2) environmental control levels are monitored at the defined frequency — with current sensor readings, threshold alert configurations, and monitoring history available as evidence.

NIST-1483.description: Provide environmental monitoring configuration and records confirming: (1) automated environmental control monitoring is employed; and (2) the monitoring system provides an alarm or notification to defined personnel or roles when conditions change in ways potentially harmful to personnel or equipment — with sensor configuration, alert setup, and a sample alert from the current period.

NIST-1484.description: Provide physical protection inspection records confirming: (1) master shutoff or isolation valves are in place to protect the system from water damage; and (2) these valves are accessible, in working order, and their location is known to key organizational personnel — with inspection records and a list of personnel with documented knowledge of valve locations.

NIST-1485.description: Provide water detection configuration and records confirming: (1) the presence of water near the system can be automatically detected; and (2) service provider building maintenance/physical security personnel are automatically alerted via defined automated mechanisms — with sensor placement documentation, alert configuration, and a sample alert or test record.

NIST-1486.description: Provide delivery authorization and inspection records confirming all system components are authorized and inspected before entering the facility — with documentation of approvals, inspection completion, and custody transfer.

NIST-1487.description: Provide removal authorization records confirming all system components are approved and documented before exiting the facility — with the authorizing official's identity and approval date recorded.

NIST-1488.description: Provide system component delivery and removal records confirming all component movements are documented and maintained.

NIST-1489.description: Provide records confirming authorized alternate work sites are identified and documented, with any associated security requirements for those locations specified.

NIST-1490.description: Provide records confirming defined security controls are actively employed at alternate work sites — such as endpoint protection requirements, VPN enforcement, and physical security guidance for remote personnel.

NIST-1491.description: Provide alternate work site assessment records confirming the effectiveness of security controls is evaluated at defined intervals.

NIST-1492.description: Provide physical access records confirming a means for employees to communicate with information security and privacy personnel in case of incidents is provided.

NIST-1493.description: Provide facility layout documentation and records confirming system components are positioned within the facility to minimize potential damage from physical and environmental hazards identified during the threat assessment — and to minimize opportunity for unauthorized access — with component placement documentation and the threat assessment driving the positioning decisions.

NIST-1495.description: Provide distribution records confirming copies of the SSP and applicable plans are provided to the Chief Privacy Officer, ISSO, and other defined personnel, with updates distributed after any changes.

NIST-1496.description: Provide plan review records confirming the SSP and applicable plans are reviewed at least annually and updated as required.

NIST-1497.description: Provide records confirming security and privacy plans are updated to reflect system and environment of operations changes, problems identified during implementation or assessments, and problems encountered during control assessments.

NIST-1498.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming plans are protected from unauthorized disclosure and modification.

NIST-1499.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming rules that describe responsibilities and expected behavior for information and system usage, security, and privacy are established for and provided to individuals requiring access to the system.

NIST-1500.description: Provide records and supporting evidence confirming before authorizing access to information and the system, a documented acknowledgement from such individuals indicating that they have read, understand, and agree to abide by the rules of behavior is r.

NIST-1501.description: Provide Rules of Behavior (or equivalent acceptable use policy) review records confirming the rules are reviewed, updated, and re-acknowledged by applicable personnel at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements. Include the current approved Rules of Behavior, review date, approval evidence, and distribution or acknowledgment records.

NIST-1502.description: Provide records confirming individuals who acknowledged a previous version of the rules of behavior are required to re-read and re-acknowledge them at least annually and whenever the rules are revised or changed.

NIST-1503.description: Provide the Rules of Behavior confirming they include explicit restrictions on: (1) the use of social media, social networking sites, and external sites or applications; (2) posting organizational information to public websites; and (3) using organizational identifiers and credentials for creating personal accounts on external systems.

NIST-1505.description: Provide the security and privacy architecture documentation confirming all required elements are addressed: (1) the security architecture describes the requirements and approach for protecting the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of organizational information; (2) the privacy architecture describes the requirements and approach for processing personally identifiable information to minimize privacy risk to individuals; (3) both architectures describe how they are integrated into and support the enterprise architecture; and (4) both architectures describe any assumptions about and dependencies on external systems and services.

NIST-1506.description: Provide records confirming changes in the enterprise architecture are reviewed and the security and privacy architectures are updated to reflect those changes at least annually and whenever a significant change occurs, with update dates and change-specific references as evidence.

NIST-1510.description: Provide system configuration export confirming a control baseline for the system is selected.

NIST-1511.description: Provide system configuration export confirming the selected control baseline is tailored by applying specified tailoring actions.

NIST-1512.description: Provide position risk designations confirming each position has an assigned risk level based on its access and responsibilities.

NIST-1513.description: Provide personnel security records confirming screening criteria are established for individuals filling organizational positions.

NIST-1514.description: Provide position risk designation records (or equivalent role-based sensitivity or trust classification records) confirming designations are reviewed and updated at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, with the current designation records, review date, and documentation of any changes made during the review period.

NIST-1515.description: Provide personnel security records confirming individuals are screened prior to authorizing access to the system.

NIST-1516.description: Provide re-screening records confirming individuals with national security clearances are re-investigated on required schedules — including during the fifth (5th) year for Top Secret security clearances, during the tenth (10th) year for Secret security clearances, and at any other defined conditions requiring re-screening per organizational policy — with completion records, re-screening authorization documentation, and evidence the process is applied consistently to all applicable individuals holding clearances.

NIST-1517.description: Provide personnel security records confirming individuals accessing systems that process, store, or transmit information requiring special protection: (1) have valid access authorizations demonstrated through a formal documented need-to-know process appropriate to the information type; and (2) satisfy additional personnel screening criteria specific to that information type — with both authorization and screening completion documented per individual.

NIST-1519.description: Provide access revocation records confirming system access is disabled upon employee termination within the timeframe required by the applicable framework. Include ticket records, access revocation logs, and timestamps confirming the required revocation window was met for terminations occurring during the review period.

NIST-1520.description: Provide exit interview records confirming a discussion of defined security topics was conducted upon termination.

NIST-1521.description: Provide termination records confirming access to organizational information formerly controlled by the terminated individual is retained and reassigned appropriately.

NIST-1523.description: Provide termination notification records and automated mechanism configuration confirming that upon employee termination: (1) defined automated mechanisms are used to notify access control personnel responsible for disabling system access — with configuration evidence identifying the automated notification mechanism; and (2) notification records confirming the defined personnel or roles were notified with timestamps, recipient roles, and confirmation that notification occurred within the required timeframe for each termination event in the review period.

NIST-1524.description: Provide records confirming that when individuals are reassigned or transferred to other positions, the ongoing operational need for their current logical and physical access authorizations to systems and facilities is reviewed and confirmed — with transfer records showing access was evaluated, adjusted, or terminated as appropriate.

NIST-1525.description: Provide transfer records confirming required reassignment actions — including access reviews and adjustments — are initiated within 24 hours of the formal transfer action.

NIST-1526.description: Provide transfer records confirming access was reviewed and updated to reflect the new role and any unnecessary access was removed.

NIST-1527.description: Provide transfer notification records confirming access control personnel and other defined roles are notified within 24 hours of a personnel transfer or reassignment.

NIST-1528.description: Provide personnel security records confirming access agreements are developed and documented for organizational systems.

NIST-1529.description: Provide access agreement review records confirming all access agreements are reviewed and updated at least annually, with the review date, reviewer identity, and any resulting changes documented.

NIST-1530.description: Provide personnel security records confirming individuals requiring access to organizational information and systems sign appropriate access agreements prior to being granted access.

NIST-1531.description: Provide personnel security records confirming individuals re-sign access agreements at least annually and any time there is a change to the user's level of access, to maintain access to organizational systems when agreements have been updated or revised.

NIST-1533.description: Provide personnel security records confirming personnel security requirements are established, including security roles and responsibilities for external providers.

NIST-1534.description: Provide third-party contracts or agreements confirming external service providers are explicitly required to comply with the organization's personnel security policies and procedures, with contractual compliance monitoring records from the current period as evidence.

NIST-1535.description: Provide records confirming external service providers notify appropriate organizational personnel — including access control and facility security personnel — when their employees who possess organizational credentials or system access are terminated (immediately) or transferred (within twenty-four (24) hours), with notification records confirming timely execution per FedRAMP requirements.

NIST-1536.description: Provide personnel security records confirming provider compliance with personnel security requirements is monitored.

NIST-1537.description: Provide personnel security records confirming a formal sanctions process is employed for individuals failing to comply with established information security and privacy policies and procedures.

NIST-1538.description: Provide sanctions process records confirming that when a formal employee sanctions process is initiated, the ISSO or equivalent role is notified within 24 hours — identifying the individual sanctioned and the reason for the notification. Evidence must include both the sanctions record and the corresponding timely notification to security personnel.

NIST-1539.description: Provide personnel security records confirming security and privacy roles and responsibilities are incorporated into organizational position descriptions.

NIST-1540.description: Provide the FIPS 199 security categorization documentation confirming the system and the information it processes, stores, and transmits are categorized with management approval.

NIST-1541.description: Provide the security categorization approval documentation confirming the authorizing official or designated representative reviewed and approved the security categorization decision — such as a signed FIPS 199, ATO letter referencing the categorization, or equivalent approval record.

NIST-1542.description: Provide risk assessment records confirming a risk assessment is conducted to identify threats to and vulnerabilities in the system, including the likelihood and magnitude of harm from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modificat.

NIST-1543.description: Provide risk assessment records confirming a risk assessment is conducted to determine the likelihood and impact of adverse effects on individuals arising from the processing of personally identifiable information.

NIST-1544.description: Provide risk assessment records confirming risk assessment results and risk management decisions from the organization and mission or business process perspectives are integrated with system-level risk assessments.

NIST-1545.description: Provide the Security Assessment Report (SAR) or equivalent risk assessment documentation confirming risk assessment results are formally documented in the security assessment report — with identified threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, impact, and risk determinations recorded.

NIST-1546.description: Provide risk assessment records confirming risk assessment results and risk management decisions are disseminated to defined personnel or roles at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements and whenever significant changes occur. Include current distribution records and evidence of management review or approval.

NIST-1547.description: Provide risk assessment dissemination records confirming results are shared with all Authorizing Officials and other defined personnel after each assessment or update.

NIST-1548.description: Provide risk assessment update records confirming the risk assessment is reviewed and updated at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements. Include the current risk assessment with its approval date, and evidence of the most recent periodic review.

NIST-1549.description: Provide supply chain risk assessment records confirming risks associated with defined systems, components, and services are assessed — covering supplier reliability, component integrity, and potential adversarial compromise — with assessment results and findings documented.

NIST-1550.description: Provide records confirming the supply chain risk assessment is reviewed and updated at the defined frequency and when significant changes to the supply chain, system, environment, or threat landscape indicate a reassessment is warranted.

NIST-1553.description: Provide authenticated vulnerability scan reports from the current period confirming all in-scope components are scanned at FedRAMP-required frequencies. Reports must be provided separately for each of the following scan types: (1) OS/infrastructure — monthly; (2) web applications (including APIs) — monthly; (3) databases — monthly; and (4) containers — monthly if containers are deployed; if no containers are in use, explicitly state that in the evidence write-up. Each scan type must include the scan results, scope, and execution date as separate supporting artifacts. Confirm the scanning tools: (a) employ interoperability standards to facilitate data exchange among tools; (b) use standard enumeration formats for platforms, software flaws, and improper configurations (e.g., CVE/NVD); (c) use standard formats for checklists and test procedures (e.g., XCCDF/OVAL); and (d) measure and report vulnerability impact using CVSS v3 scores.

NIST-1554.description: Provide two distinct sets of evidence: (1) vulnerability scan result analysis confirming findings are reviewed and used to inform remediation priorities; and (2) separately, security controls assessment result analysis showing how assessment outcomes are evaluated. These are distinct activities and must each be evidenced independently.

NIST-1555.description: Provide vulnerability remediation records confirming findings are resolved within FedRAMP-required timeframes by severity: Critical and High — within 30 days of discovery; Moderate — within 90 days; Low — within 180 days. Note: any vulnerability listed in the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog must be remediated within the KEV-specified due date, which may be shorter than standard FedRAMP timeframes. Include the vulnerability identification date and remediation date for each finding.

NIST-1556.description: Provide records confirming vulnerability monitoring results and control assessment findings are shared with defined personnel or roles to help eliminate similar vulnerabilities and identify systemic weaknesses, with distribution records from the current period showing recipients and sharing dates.

NIST-1557.description: Provide the vulnerability scanning tool configuration confirming scan capabilities are updated to detect the latest known vulnerabilities.

NIST-1558.description: Provide documentation confirming the breadth and depth of vulnerability scanning coverage are defined — specifying which system components, network segments, and application layers are in scope, what scanning depth is required (e.g., authenticated scanning), and any documented coverage thresholds or exclusions with justification.

NIST-1559.description: Provide discoverable information assessment records confirming: (1) information about the system discoverable by external parties has been assessed; and (2) when discoverable information is confirmed, appropriate service provider personnel are notified and defined corrective actions are followed — with the assessment methodology, findings, and corrective action records.

NIST-1560.description: Provide scan configuration and output artifacts clearly confirming ALL scans were run with privileged/authenticated credentials. For each scan, show the credential or account used. If any scans could not be run as privileged, explicitly identify which scans were not privileged and explain why, rather than omitting the detail.

NIST-1561.description: Provide historic audit log review records confirming that for all high or critical vulnerability scan findings, historic audit logs have been reviewed to determine whether the identified vulnerability has been previously exploited — with the audit log review methodology, timeframe reviewed, and findings documented per high/critical finding.

NIST-1563.description: Provide the Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP) or equivalent public reporting channel documentation confirming a channel is established for external parties to report vulnerabilities in organizational systems and components — including the channel's address or URL, the defined scope, and any acknowledgment or response commitment.

NIST-1564.description: Provide risk response records confirming findings from security assessments, privacy assessments, continuous monitoring, and audits are tracked and responded to in accordance with organizational risk tolerance — such as POA&M entries, remediation records, or risk acceptance decisions with approving authority documented.

NIST-1565.description: Provide the criticality analysis identifying critical system components and functions, with evidence the analysis was performed at defined decision points and results inform acquisition and protection decisions.

NIST-1567.description: Provide acquisition and development planning records confirming all three of the following are addressed for the system or service: (1) high-level security and privacy requirements are determined as part of mission and business process planning — not added after acquisition decisions are made; (2) the resources required to protect the system or service are determined, documented, and allocated as part of the organizational capital planning and investment control process — with budget line items or resource allocation evidence; and (3) discrete line items for both information security and privacy are established in organizational programming and budgeting documentation — confirming security and privacy funding is visible and tracked separately from general IT investment.

NIST-1568.description: Provide SDLC documentation and supporting records confirming security and privacy are fully integrated throughout the system development lifecycle. Evidence must address all four elements: (1) SDLC definition — the system is acquired, developed, and managed using a defined SDLC that explicitly integrates both security and privacy considerations at each phase, from requirements through disposal; (2) role definition — information security and privacy roles and responsibilities are defined and documented throughout the SDLC, with role assignments reflected in SDLC artifacts such as the SSP, project plans, or developer contracts; (3) individual identification — individuals assigned information security and privacy roles are identified and actively included in SDLC activities at each applicable phase; and (4) risk management integration — organizational information security and privacy risk management processes are integrated into SDLC lifecycle activities, with acquisition and development records confirming this integration is in practice, not just documented in policy.

NIST-1571.description: Provide acquisition contracts confirming the following requirements are explicitly included or incorporated by reference for all applicable procurements — covering both security and privacy throughout: (a) functional requirements describing the required security and privacy capabilities; (b) strength of mechanism requirements specifying the required robustness level for security and privacy functions; (c) design and implementation information requirements covering architecture, interfaces, and implementation details; (d) assurance requirements establishing the evidence needed to confirm controls are correctly implemented; (e) documentation requirements specifying what security and privacy documentation contractors must produce and deliver; (f) requirements for protecting, controlling, and not publicly disclosing security and privacy documentation provided during acquisition; (g) requirements for the system development environment and the intended operational environment; (h) allocation of responsibility identifying the parties accountable for each security and privacy function; and (i) acceptance criteria defining the conditions that must be met before the system, component, or service is accepted — with all elements clearly addressed in reviewed contract language.

NIST-1572.description: Provide the developer-supplied documentation package for the system, component, or service confirming it covers all of the following: (1) functional properties of the security controls implemented, describing what each control does and how it behaves; (2) design and implementation information including security-relevant external interfaces, high-level design, low-level design, and source code or hardware design as applicable; and (3) all functions, ports, protocols, and services required for the system, component, or service to operate — with current documentation reflecting the production environment.

NIST-1574.description: Provide acquisition contract and developer configuration records confirming: (1) the developer is required to deliver the system, component, or service with security configurations implemented as the default — at DoD IL5, security configurations must align with applicable requirements from DoDI 8510.01 and applicable STIGs/SRGs as the baseline default configuration; (2) these configurations are used as the default for any subsequent system reinstallation or upgrade — with developer contract requirements referencing STIG/SRG compliance, delivered configuration baseline documentation, and delivery verification records confirming STIG-compliant defaults were applied.

NIST-1576.description: Provide records confirming only FIPS 201-approved products from the approved products list are used for PIV capability within organizational systems.

NIST-1577.description: Provide the complete system documentation package confirming all required documentation is present, current, and distributed. Evidence must address the following elements: Administrator documentation — (1) secure configuration, installation procedures, and operational guidance for administrative functions, all current and reflecting the production environment; (2) effective use of security functions, known vulnerabilities, and available security configuration options; (3) effective use of privacy functions and available privacy configuration options; and (4) known vulnerabilities in administrative interfaces, including any environment-specific security considerations. User documentation — (5) user-accessible security and privacy functions and how to effectively use them; (6) interaction methods that enable users to operate the system in a manner that provides both security and privacy protection; and (7) user responsibilities for maintaining the security of the system and the privacy of individuals whose information is processed. Documentation availability and distribution — (8) when documentation is unavailable or nonexistent, attempts to obtain it are documented with what was sought, from whom, the outcome, and any compensating actions taken; and (9) documentation including security-relevant documentation is distributed to the ISSO or equivalent role at minimum, with recipients and distribution dates recorded.

NIST-1578.description: Provide system design and development records confirming both security and privacy engineering principles are applied across the full system lifecycle — from initial specification and design through development, implementation, and modification — with evidence of principle application integrated into the development artifacts: security engineering principles addressing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and accountability; and privacy engineering principles addressing data minimization, transparency, and privacy-by-design — both documented consistently across lifecycle phases and reflected in design documentation, code reviews, or equivalent artifacts.

NIST-1583.description: Provide the external system service inventory confirming all third-party services have been reviewed against FedRAMP authorization requirements. The inventory must be comprehensive — drawing from the SSP service tables, system boundary diagrams, and a thorough review of services actively in use (for example, monitoring tools, code repositories, ticketing systems, and collaboration platforms). Reference every service by its exact name as listed on the FedRAMP Marketplace. For each service not authorized at the correct impact level, provide: (1) the exact FedRAMP Marketplace service name; (2) a detailed description of what federal data or metadata is present on that system — what kind of data, where it originated, and where it ends up; and (3) screenshots showing the data present on the system. Also provide ongoing oversight records confirming third-party service providers are monitored for continued compliance. For any service not at the correct authorization level, include the compensating controls or formally accepted risk.

NIST-1584.description: Provide acquisition and development records confirming: (a) an organizational assessment of risk is conducted prior to the acquisition or outsourcing of information security services; and (b) at DoD IL5, the acquisition or outsourcing of dedicated information security services is approved by the DoD Component CIO or their delegate(s) — with risk assessment documentation, CIO or delegate approval records, and evidence the approval process is applied consistently to all relevant security service acquisitions.

NIST-1585.description: Provide acquisition and development records confirming providers of all external systems where federal customer data is processed or stored are required to identify the functions, ports, protocols, and other services required for the use of such services — with contractual requirements and provider-supplied documentation as evidence.

NIST-1587.description: Provide the external service configuration confirming information processing, data, and system services are restricted to U.S. and U.S. territories geographic locations — at DoD IL5, ALL THREE categories (information processing, information or data, AND system services) must be restricted, not just a subset — based on defined requirements or conditions. Evidence must include: provider contracts or SLAs explicitly restricting processing and storage to U.S./U.S. territories; technical configuration controls enforcing the geographic restriction; and confirmation that no component of the service operates outside U.S. legal jurisdiction.

NIST-1591.description: Provide developer configuration management records confirming system changes are tracked, reviewed, and integrity-verified.
