NIST-1592.description: Provide developer configuration management records confirming changes to defined configuration items are documented, managed, and integrity-verified throughout the development lifecycle.

NIST-1593.description: Provide developer change management records confirming only organization-approved changes are implemented in the system, component, or service.

NIST-1594.description: Provide developer change documentation confirming approved changes, potential security impacts of those changes, and any security flaws in the implementation are all recorded.

NIST-1595.description: Provide developer flaw tracking records confirming security flaws are tracked, resolutions are documented, and findings are reported to defined personnel.

NIST-1599.description: Provide the developer's security and privacy testing plans — the documents defining the testing approach, scope, and methods for both security and privacy as part of the SDLC. These are not the SSP and are not test results. Confirm both plans are implemented and current, and reference each actual plan artifact by name. The developer must be required at all post-design lifecycle stages to develop, implement, and maintain plans for ongoing security and privacy assessments.

NIST-1600.description: Provide the actual security test RESULTS for unit, integration, system, and/or regression testing conducted during the development lifecycle. Reference the specific test result reports — not the test plan and not the SSP.

NIST-1601.description: Provide developer evidence confirming security testing was performed at all post-design lifecycle stages, with test evidence — not just a plan — showing testing was conducted and results documented.

NIST-1602.description: Provide records confirming the developer implemented a verifiable flaw remediation process, with evidence that identified security defects have corresponding remediation records and are tracked to closure.

NIST-1603.description: Provide developer records confirming flaws identified during testing are corrected before deployment, with remediation documentation showing the defect, the fix applied, and verification testing results.

NIST-1604.description: Provide actual static code analysis reports or screenshots confirming static analysis was performed on the codebase. Reference the specific tool used and the output generated — not just a statement that static analysis is part of the SDLC process.

NIST-1605.description: Provide developer threat modeling and vulnerability analysis records confirming analyses were conducted using defined impact, environment, and threat information during both the development phase and subsequent testing and evaluation phases — with records from each phase available separately.

NIST-1606.description: Provide records confirming the developer performed both threat modeling and vulnerability analyses during development and subsequent testing and evaluation using CSP-defined tools and methods — with the specific tools, methods, and outputs documented.

NIST-1607.description: Provide developer records confirming both threat modeling and vulnerability analyses were performed at the CSP-defined breadth and depth of modeling and analysis during development and subsequent testing and evaluation — with the scope and coverage documented.

NIST-1608.description: Provide developer records confirming both threat modeling results and vulnerability analysis results were evaluated against CSP-defined acceptance criteria — with findings addressed and acceptance criteria explicitly referenced in the results documentation.

NIST-1609.description: Provide the developer's documented development process confirming both security and privacy requirements are explicitly addressed throughout the development lifecycle.

NIST-1610.description: Provide the developer's documented development process confirming it identifies the standards and tools used in development.

NIST-1611.description: Provide the developer's documented development process confirming specific tools and tool configurations used in development are documented.

NIST-1612.description: Provide the developer's change management documentation confirming changes to the development process, tools, and configurations are documented, managed, and integrity-verified.

NIST-1613.description: Provide developer security test and evaluation records confirming a security assessment plan or equivalent test plan is reviewed and agreed upon before first use and at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements thereafter. Include review records, approval signatures, and evidence of compliance with applicable authorization or assessment requirements.

NIST-1614.description: Provide records confirming the developer performed a criticality analysis at defined decision points during the development lifecycle, with analysis results available at the defined breadth and depth.

NIST-1616.description: Provide developer training documentation confirming the developer of the system, component, or service is required to provide training on the correct use and operation of implemented security and privacy functions — with contract requirements specifying training obligations and training delivery records.

NIST-1617.description: Provide the developer security and privacy architecture documentation confirming the developer is required to produce design specifications and architecture documentation that: (1) are consistent with the organization's security and privacy architectures; (2) accurately and completely describe the required security and privacy functionality and allocation of controls; and (3) express how individual security and privacy functions, mechanisms, and services work together — covering both security and privacy architecture elements.

NIST-1618.description: Provide developer screening records confirming the developer of defined systems, components, or services: (1) has appropriate access authorizations as determined by assigned official government duties; and (2) satisfies defined additional personnel screening criteria — with screening documentation and access authorization records.

NIST-1619.description: Provide acquisition and development records confirming the organization manages unsupported system components through both replacement and alternative support: (1) components are replaced when vendor, developer, or manufacturer support is no longer available — with replacement plans or records confirming end-of-life components are actively tracked and replaced; and (2) where immediate replacement is not feasible, options for alternative support are defined and available before end-of-support dates — such as in-house support arrangements, third-party maintenance agreements, or equivalent alternatives — with documentation confirming these options are evaluated and in place.

NIST-1620.description: Provide the system architecture documentation and configuration confirming user-facing functionality is separated from system management functionality.

NIST-1621.description: Provide the security function isolation configuration confirming security functions are isolated from non-security functions — through separate execution domains, memory partitioning, or equivalent mechanisms — with system architecture documentation and configuration evidence confirming the isolation is enforced.

NIST-1622.description: Provide configuration and architecture evidence demonstrating: (1) how customer data is separated from other tenants' data — describe the specific mechanism, such as separate databases, separate storage buckets, separate virtual networks, or tenant-scoped access controls; and (2) how customer data is separated from CSP internal data — describe the data flow boundary between customer-facing and CSP-internal systems.

NIST-1623.description: Provide the DoS protection configuration confirming the system is protected against defined attack types — at minimum ICMP flood, SYN flood, slowloris, buffer overflow, and volume attacks per FedRAMP requirements.

NIST-1624.description: Provide the denial-of-service protection configuration confirming mitigations are in place for the defined attack vectors.

NIST-1625.description: Provide the external boundary protection configuration and evidence, covering only external-facing mechanisms — such as a web application firewall, network intrusion detection, VPN gateway, bastion host, or SIEM ingesting external traffic. Address each external mechanism separately and distinctly from internal controls.

NIST-1626.description: Provide the internal boundary protection configuration and evidence, covering only internal mechanisms — such as host-based intrusion detection, internal network traffic monitoring, or internal SIEM feeds. Address each internal mechanism separately and distinctly from external boundary controls.

NIST-1627.description: Provide the network segmentation configuration confirming subnets or equivalent network segments are implemented to logically separate publicly accessible components from internal components. Include routing evidence confirming traffic passes through the appropriate managed interfaces at the boundary.

NIST-1628.description: Provide the managed interface inventory and configuration clearly identifying which interfaces are in place — by name and type. Identify the network segments or virtual networks (e.g., subnets, VLANs, VPCs) in which each managed interface resides. Include routing table artifacts confirming all traffic — including remote access connections — entering or exiting the authorization boundary passes through these managed interfaces with no direct external connections that bypass them. Confirm a default-deny posture is enforced at managed interfaces — all communications traffic is denied unless explicitly permitted, with each permit rule having documented justification.

NIST-1629.description: Provide the network architecture and firewall configuration confirming the number of external network connections is limited and each connection is managed through a controlled interface.

NIST-1630.description: Provide the managed interface configuration confirming a managed interface — such as a firewall, gateway, or border router — is implemented and operational for each external telecommunication service, with the interface identified by name and type for each external telecom connection.

NIST-1631.description: Provide the traffic flow policy confirming a documented policy is established for each managed interface — defining which traffic is permitted, under what conditions, and with what approval basis.

NIST-1632.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming the confidentiality and integrity of the information being transmitted across each interface is protected.

NIST-1633.description: Provide records confirming each exception to the traffic flow policy is documented with: (1) a supporting mission or business need that justifies the exception; and (2) the duration for which the exception is authorized — with expired exceptions removed or re-authorized.

NIST-1634.description: Provide firewall or network traffic flow policy review records confirming reviews are conducted at a frequency consistent with the applicable framework's requirements, or whenever there is a change in the threat environment that warrants a review. Include the review date, scope, findings, and documentation of any resulting rule set changes.

NIST-1635.description: Provide the network configuration confirming unauthorized control plane traffic exchanges with external networks are prevented.

NIST-1636.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming information is published to enable remote networks to detect unauthorized control plane traffic from internal networks.

NIST-1637.description: Provide the boundary filtering configuration confirming control plane traffic from external networks is actively filtered and blocked from reaching internal systems — with firewall or ACL rules specifically addressing unauthorized control plane protocols.

NIST-1638.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming split tunneling is prevented for remote devices connecting to organizational systems unless the split tunnel is securely provisioned.

NIST-1639.description: Provide the proxy configuration confirming defined internal communications destined for external networks are routed through authenticated proxy servers at managed interfaces, with no direct external connections that bypass the proxy.

NIST-1641.description: Provide the data exfiltration prevention configuration and test records confirming: (1) the system employs controls to prevent exfiltration of information through defined mechanisms; and (2) exfiltration tests are conducted at the defined frequency — with DLP or equivalent configuration evidence and test results.

NIST-1643.description: Provide the host-based boundary protection configuration confirming HIPS/HIDS or equivalent host-based controls are deployed on all defined system components.

NIST-1647.description: Provide system configuration and access records confirming systems are prevented from entering unsecure states in the event of an operational failure of a boundary protection device.

NIST-1648.description: Provide the dynamic isolation configuration confirming the capability exists to dynamically isolate defined system components from other components — enabling rapid containment of compromised components — with network architecture documentation and configuration evidence confirming dynamic isolation can be executed.

NIST-1649.description: Provide the mission/function isolation configuration confirming boundary protection mechanisms are employed to isolate defined system components supporting defined missions and/or business functions from each other — with architecture documentation and configuration evidence confirming mission-function separation is enforced.

NIST-1654.description: Provide network-layer session termination configuration records confirming inactive sessions are automatically terminated at the network level — distinct from application-layer session termination — after no more than an organization-defined period of inactivity. Include configuration output from all applicable network and system components confirming the threshold is enforced, with separate evidence addressing privileged and standard user session timeouts where the framework distinguishes between them.

NIST-1655.description: Provide the cryptographic key management configuration confirming keys are generated, distributed, stored, accessed, and destroyed in accordance with Federal requirements, with a current key management plan or equivalent documentation available.

NIST-1656.description: Provide key escrow documentation confirming cryptographic key availability is maintained in the event of key loss — with: (1) the escrow agreement, policy, or equivalent arrangement documenting the method and scope of key escrow; (2) designation of the escrow custodian or trusted third-party arrangement; (3) defined procedures for key recovery, including authorization requirements for recovery requests; (4) records confirming the escrow arrangement is active and operationally tested — ensuring cryptographic information remains available even when individual user keys are lost or inaccessible.

NIST-1658.description: Provide a complete cryptographic use inventory and implementation evidence confirming all cryptographic uses within the system boundary are identified and each is implemented using FIPS-validated or NSA-approved mechanisms. Note that this control covers all cryptographic functions — not only encryption — including hashing, random number generation, key generation, and protocols such as TLS, SSH, and HTTPS. The inventory must organize all uses into the following categories, with evidence for each addressed separately: (1) Data at Rest (DaR) — identify every storage component where federal data or metadata resides — including databases, file systems, object storage, disk volumes, and backup files — and confirm each uses FIPS 140-2 validated encryption with the corresponding NIST CMVP certificate number. Password and credential storage must use approved hashing or encryption — not plaintext; (2) Data in Transit (DiT) — confirm ALL data transmission paths use FIPS-validated cryptographic modules, including remote access sessions since all access to a cloud system traverses external networks. Provide the NIST CMVP certificate number for each module and confirm only TLS 1.2 or higher is permitted across all communication channels. Password transmission must only occur over encrypted channels; (3) MFA — confirm the cryptographic mechanisms supporting multi-factor authentication use FIPS-validated or NSA-approved modules — including that any MFA device meets the defined cryptographic strength requirements — and provide the corresponding NIST CMVP certificate number for each; (4) Other — capture any remaining cryptographic uses not covered above (e.g., OTP generation, digital signatures, key derivation); and (5) provide artifacts confirming all applicable endpoints are configured to operate in FIPS mode, along with the completed FedRAMP Cryptographic Module table reflecting all modules in use. For each module across all categories, provide its correct name, the associated service or use case, its active NIST CMVP certificate number, and flag any historical, non-active, or missing certificates with the associated risk.

NIST-1659.description: Provide the configuration confirming remote activation of all collaborative computing devices — cameras, microphones, and application sharing — is prohibited with no exceptions, as required by FedRAMP. Where physical disablement is used, confirm it is enforced.

NIST-1660.description: Provide a screenshot or system demonstration confirming an explicit active-use indicator is displayed to users physically present at a device when collaborative computing capabilities are in use.

NIST-1665.description: Provide the PKI certificate management documentation confirming: (a) public key certificates are issued under a defined certificate policy or obtained from an approved service provider — at DoD IL5, per DoDI 8520.02 (Public Key Infrastructure and Public Key Enabling), DoD PKI certificates are required; and (b) only approved trust anchors are included in organization-managed trust stores or certificate stores — with certificate policy documentation, approved CA list, trust store configuration, and evidence that no unapproved CAs are present in any system trust store. DoD IL5 requires PKI compliance in accordance with DoDI 8520.02.

NIST-1666.description: Provide the mobile code authorization records and monitoring configuration confirming only approved mobile code technologies are used and actively monitored within the system.

NIST-1671.description: Provide the DNSSEC configuration for the authoritative DNS service, confirming: (1) the DNS service in use is identified by name and is FedRAMP authorized (for example, a FedRAMP-authorized managed DNS service); (2) all system URLs are listed; and (3) each URL has been tested on DNSviz.net with the specific result stated — results containing 'Error', 'Bogus', or 'Insecure' indicate DNSSEC failure; all other results indicate a passing status.

NIST-1672.description: Provide configuration confirming the authoritative DNS service provides data origin authentication indicators along with DNS responses. Include DNSviz.net test results for each system URL with the pass or fail status explicitly stated.

NIST-1673.description: Provide configuration confirming the authoritative DNS service enables verification of a chain of trust in DNS responses. Include DNSviz.net test results for each system URL with the pass or fail status explicitly stated.

NIST-1674.description: Provide the DNSSEC configuration for the recursive or caching DNS resolver, confirming: (1) the DNS service in use is identified by name and is FedRAMP authorized; (2) all system URLs are listed; and (3) each URL has been tested on DNSviz.net with the specific result stated — 'Error', 'Bogus', or 'Insecure' indicates failure; all other results indicate a passing status.

NIST-1675.description: Provide the DNS architecture documentation confirming name/address resolution services are fault-tolerant and implement both internal and external role separation.

NIST-1676.description: Provide the session management configuration confirming session authenticity is protected — for example through TLS, session tokens, and session invalidation upon logout.

NIST-1680.description: Provide the fail-secure configuration confirming defined types of system failures on defined system components result in transition to a defined known secure state while preserving defined system state information necessary to determine the cause of failure — per DoD IL5 requirements — with failure mode documentation, system configuration evidence identifying the known secure state for each failure type, test records confirming fail-secure behavior is validated, and evidence that state information is preserved and accessible for post-failure analysis.

NIST-1683.description: Provide the operating system configuration confirming separate execution domains are maintained for each running process through isolation mechanisms such as virtual address space separation, preventing unauthorized information transfer between processes.

NIST-1684.description: Provide the time synchronization configuration confirming all system clocks are synchronized to an authoritative time source at the required frequency, with NTP configuration and synchronization logs as evidence.

NIST-1685.description: Provide the NTP configuration confirming primary and secondary time servers are from the NIST Internet Time Service (tf.nist.gov), with the secondary server from a different geographic region, and that all system clocks synchronize at least hourly. Coverage must extend to all platforms — including non-Windows systems and network devices — confirming each synchronizes to the Windows Server Domain Controller PDC emulator or directly to the authoritative NIST source. Include evidence that internal system clocks are used as the authoritative timestamp source for all audit records, with NTP synchronization logs confirming accuracy.

NIST-1687.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming system flaws are identified, reported and corrected.

NIST-1688.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming software and firmware updates related to flaw remediation are tested for effectiveness and potential side effects before installation.

NIST-1689.description: Provide patch management records confirming security-relevant software and firmware updates are installed within 30 days of release per FedRAMP requirements, with patch deployment dates confirming timely execution.

NIST-1690.description: Provide configuration management records confirming flaw remediation is integrated into the organizational CM process — such as patch management procedures, change request records that include vulnerability findings as the trigger, or a CM policy section explicitly governing flaw-driven changes.

NIST-1691.description: Provide automated patch deployment tool output confirming applicable security-relevant software and firmware updates are installed on all in-scope system components at least monthly per FedRAMP requirements using automated mechanisms — not manual processes — with deployment logs showing component coverage and installation dates.

NIST-1692.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming the time between flaw identification and flaw remediation is measured.

NIST-1693.description: Provide the flaw remediation performance benchmarks confirming the organization has established measurable targets for remediation timeframes by severity — with current performance metrics available against those benchmarks.

NIST-1696.description: Provide the malicious code protection configuration confirming both signature-based and non-signature-based mechanisms are deployed at all system entry and exit points — including network gateways, email systems, and web proxies — as well as on endpoints. Evidence must confirm both mechanism types are active, not just that a single protection tool is in place.

NIST-1697.description: Provide configuration and version records confirming malicious code protection definitions and signatures are updated automatically as new releases become available, consistent with the configuration management policy. Include evidence showing the current definition version and the last automatic update timestamp across all protected components.

NIST-1698.description: Provide the malicious code protection scan schedule configuration confirming periodic scans are configured to run at least weekly across all in-scope components, along with a recent scan execution log confirming the schedule is being honored.

NIST-1699.description: Provide the real-time scanning configuration confirming files from external sources are scanned upon download or receipt at both endpoints and network entry and exit points. Include evidence of a recent real-time scan event confirming the capability is active.

NIST-1700.description: Provide the malicious code protection configuration confirming the mechanism is deployed and active at the workstation and server level, with scan logs from the current period confirming host-level coverage across all in-scope endpoints.

NIST-1701.description: Provide the alert configuration confirming the malicious code protection mechanism is set to notify defined personnel or roles upon detection. Include a sample alert record from the current period confirming notifications are being generated and routed to the appropriate recipients.

NIST-1702.description: Provide the false positive handling configuration and any records from the current period confirming the organization has a defined process for addressing false positive detections. Include evidence that potential availability impacts from false positives — such as quarantined legitimate files — are assessed and managed.

NIST-1704.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming the system is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

NIST-1705.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming the system is monitored to detect unauthorized local, network and remote connections.

NIST-1706.description: Provide the system monitoring technique and method documentation confirming the specific approaches used to identify unauthorized system use — such as user behavior analytics, anomaly detection, or audit log correlation — with active monitoring evidence from the current period.

NIST-1707.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming internal monitoring capabilities are invoked or monitoring devices are deployed strategically within the system to collect organization-determined essential information.

NIST-1708.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming monitoring devices or capabilities are deployed at ad hoc or strategic locations within the system to track specific transaction types of interest, with deployment evidence and sample monitoring output.

NIST-1709.description: Provide log analysis records from the current period confirming detected events and anomalies are analyzed and escalated to appropriate personnel.

NIST-1710.description: Provide records confirming the system monitoring level is adjusted in response to changes in organizational risk, with evidence of increased monitoring during elevated risk periods.

NIST-1711.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming a legal opinion regarding system monitoring activities is obtained.

NIST-1712.description: Provide records confirming system monitoring information is shared with defined personnel at the required frequency — with a sample distribution or monitoring report from the current period as evidence.

NIST-1713.description: Provide system configuration export confirming individual intrusion detection tools are connected and configured to a system-wide intrusion detection system.

NIST-1714.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming automated tools and mechanisms are employed to support a near real-time analysis of events.

NIST-1715.description: Provide the system monitoring policy, configuration, or SIEM rule documentation confirming that criteria for detecting unusual or unauthorized inbound and outbound communications traffic are defined — specifying the patterns, thresholds, or behavioral indicators that constitute anomalous activity requiring investigation or automated alerting.

NIST-1716.description: Provide the network monitoring configuration confirming inbound and outbound communications traffic is monitored continuously for defined unusual or unauthorized activities, with monitoring logs or alerts from the current period confirming active coverage.

NIST-1717.description: Provide the compromise indicator alerting configuration confirming defined personnel are automatically notified when system-generated compromise indicators are detected, with a sample alert from the current period.

NIST-1718.description: Provide the encrypted traffic visibility configuration confirming provisions are made so that defined encrypted communications traffic is visible to defined system monitoring tools and mechanisms — consistent with M-21-31 requirements — with TLS inspection or equivalent configuration evidence and confirmation that monitoring coverage is not degraded by encryption.

NIST-1719.description: Provide the outbound traffic anomaly detection configuration confirming outbound communications traffic at external interfaces and defined interior points is analyzed to discover anomalies — with monitoring configuration identifying both external and interior inspection points and sample anomaly detection records from the current period.

NIST-1720.description: Provide the automated monitoring alert configuration confirming defined personnel or roles are alerted using defined automated mechanisms when defined monitoring activities indicate inappropriate or unusual activity — with configuration evidence identifying alert conditions, notification targets, and sample alerts from the current period.

NIST-1721.description: Provide the wireless intrusion detection system configuration and records confirming a WIDS is deployed to identify rogue wireless devices, detect attack attempts, and detect potential compromises or breaches — with WIDS configuration, coverage documentation, and current period detection records.

NIST-1723.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming information from monitoring tools and mechanisms employed throughout the system is correlated.

NIST-1724.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming outbound communications traffic is analyzed at external system interfaces AND at defined interior points within the system to detect covert exfiltration of information — with monitoring coverage artifacts showing both boundary and interior inspection are active.

NIST-1725.description: Provide the enhanced monitoring configuration and records confirming additional monitoring measures are applied to individuals identified by defined sources as posing an increased level of risk — with the risk identification process and criteria documented, the specific additional monitoring parameters defined and enforced for those individuals, and current monitoring output for the review period demonstrating heightened scrutiny was active throughout.

NIST-1726.description: Provide the privileged user monitoring configuration and records confirming defined additional monitoring measures are implemented for all privileged users — including specific monitoring parameters applied beyond standard user monitoring, comprehensive audit logging of all privileged actions and commands, session recording or equivalent elevated scrutiny controls, and current monitoring output demonstrating the enhanced monitoring was active and producing results throughout the review period.

NIST-1727.description: Provide the unauthorized network service detection configuration confirming: (1) network services not authorized or approved through defined authorization processes are detected; and (2) defined audit or alert actions targeting defined personnel or roles are initiated when unauthorized services are detected — with detection configuration and sample detection/alert records.

NIST-1728.description: Provide the host-based monitoring configuration confirming defined monitoring mechanisms are deployed and active on all required system components, with deployment records or equivalent evidence of coverage.

NIST-1731.description: Provide records confirming security alerts, advisories, and directives are received from US-CERT and other defined sources. Include evidence CISA Emergency and Binding Operational Directives applicable to the cloud service offering are addressed per FedRAMP guidance, with implementation timelines documented.

NIST-1732.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming internal security alerts, advisories, and directives are generated as deemed necessary.

NIST-1733.description: Provide security alert dissemination records confirming alerts, advisories, and directives are distributed to system security personnel and administrators with patch/configuration management responsibilities.

NIST-1734.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming security directives are implemented in accordance with established time frames or if the issuing organization is notified of the degree of noncompliance.

NIST-1735.description: Provide the automated security alert broadcast configuration confirming defined automated mechanisms are used to broadcast security alerts and advisories throughout the organization — with configuration evidence identifying the broadcast mechanism and distribution scope, and sample alerts distributed during the review period.

NIST-1736.description: Provide security and privacy function verification records confirming defined functions are verified as operating correctly, with verification results from the current period available.

NIST-1737.description: Provide the security function verification configuration and records confirming verification runs at system startup or restart, upon privileged user command, and at least monthly — with verification results available.

NIST-1738.description: Provide the security function verification alert configuration confirming system administrators and security personnel are automatically notified when security or privacy verification tests fail, with a sample alert as evidence.

NIST-1739.description: Provide the security function anomaly response configuration confirming defined actions — system shutdown, restart, or alternative actions — are initiated when anomalies are detected.

NIST-1741.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming integrity verification tools are employed to detect unauthorized changes.

NIST-1742.description: Provide the integrity violation response configuration and records confirming defined actions are taken when unauthorized changes to software, firmware, or information are detected.

NIST-1743.description: Provide the integrity verification configuration and records confirming software and firmware integrity checks are performed at least monthly and upon security-relevant events — such as system updates, configuration changes, or detected anomalies — with check results and any findings from the current period available.

NIST-1744.description: Provide the automated integrity discrepancy notification configuration confirming automated tools provide notification to the ISSO and/or similar organizational roles upon discovering discrepancies during integrity verification — with configuration evidence and sample notifications from the current period.

NIST-1745.description: Provide the automated integrity violation response configuration confirming the system automatically performs defined actions — such as system shutdown, restart, or defined compensating controls — when integrity violations are discovered, with configuration evidence and sample response records from the current period.

NIST-1746.description: Provide records confirming that when defined security-relevant system changes are detected — such as unauthorized software modifications — these are escalated into the organizational incident response process, with an example coordination record from the current period.

NIST-1750.description: Provide the cryptographic software authentication configuration confirming FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms are implemented to authenticate all software and firmware inside the authorization boundary prior to installation — with configuration evidence and FIPS validation documentation for the cryptographic mechanisms used.

NIST-1752.description: Provide the spam protection configuration confirming: (1) spam protection mechanisms are deployed and active at all system entry and exit points to detect and act on unsolicited messages; and (2) the mechanisms are configured to update automatically when new releases become available, in accordance with organizational configuration management policies — with current version and definition timestamps as evidence.

NIST-1753.description: Provide system monitoring records confirming spam protection mechanisms are automatically updated.

NIST-1754.description: Provide a system demonstration or configuration confirming the validity of defined information inputs is checked at all applicable entry points with invalid inputs rejected. Include documentation of any approved exceptions to input validation requirements.

NIST-1758.description: Provide the error handling configuration confirming: (1) system error messages include sufficient information for corrective action but do not reveal implementation details, data paths, or other information that could be exploited by an adversary; and (2) detailed error messages are displayed only to authorized personnel — at minimum the ISSO or equivalent role — with generic messages shown to all other users.

NIST-1759.description: Provide: (1) the policy or documented standard governing how information is handled within the system — specifying retention periods, storage requirements, and disposal requirements for each applicable information type; and (2) artifacts demonstrating those requirements are being met — such as storage configuration showing retention periods, automated deletion records, or destruction certificates. A policy reference alone is not sufficient; operational evidence of compliance must accompany it.

NIST-1762.description: Provide the memory protection configuration confirming controls — such as Data Execution Prevention, Address Space Layout Randomization, or equivalent mechanisms — are implemented to prevent unauthorized code execution in system memory.

NIST-1764.description: Provide the SCRM plan confirming it addresses supply chain risk identification, assessment, and response processes with current approval and revision history.

NIST-1765.description: Provide records confirming a supply chain risk management team is established with defined personnel, roles, and responsibilities assigned, including evidence the team is actively leading SCRM activities.

NIST-1766.description: Provide supply chain risk management records confirming the organization's supply chain protection program is implemented and documented across all required elements: (1) chain-of-custody documentation confirming the integrity of all devices — including replacement devices — is verified before introduction to the authorization boundary, from procurement through deployment; (2) the process for identifying and addressing supply chain weaknesses or deficiencies is coordinated with designated supply chain personnel, with coordination documented for the current review period; (3) defined supply chain controls are actively employed to protect against supply chain risks — such as supplier vetting records, hardware integrity checks, or contractual security requirements — appropriate to the risk level of each component; and (4) selected supply chain processes and controls are documented in the applicable security plan, privacy plan, or SCRM plan, with the documentation current and approved.

NIST-1771.description: Provide acquisition records confirming defined strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods are used to protect against supply chain risks — including evidence that supply chain risk criteria are applied during procurement decisions.

NIST-1774.description: Provide the most recent supply chain risk assessment confirming risks associated with suppliers and contractors were reviewed within the past year, with findings informing procurement and oversight decisions.

NIST-1777.description: Provide the supply chain notification agreements confirming procedures are in place with supply chain entities for prompt notification of compromises, with a sample notification or agreement document as evidence.

NIST-1778.description: Provide the tamper protection program documentation confirming a tamper protection program is implemented for the system, component, or service — including documented safeguards to ensure the authenticity of software and patches supplied by vendors — with program documentation, vendor authenticity verification procedures, and current implementation evidence.

NIST-1779.description: Provide the anti-tamper technology documentation confirming anti-tamper technologies, tools, and techniques are employed throughout the system development life cycle — with SDLC policy references, anti-tamper control documentation, and evidence of implementation at relevant SDLC phases.

NIST-1780.description: Provide supply chain inspection records confirming defined systems or components are inspected at defined frequencies or upon defined triggers to detect tampering, with inspection procedures and results documented.

NIST-1781.description: Provide training records confirming defined personnel are trained to identify counterfeit system components — including hardware, software, and firmware — with training completion dates and curriculum evidence.

NIST-1782.description: Provide configuration control records confirming that system components awaiting service or repair — and components after servicing — remain under configuration management throughout the maintenance cycle, with component integrity verified upon return.

NIST-1783.description: Provide component disposal records confirming data, documentation, tools, and system components are disposed of using defined techniques and methods that meet applicable security requirements.
